Chinese has no article, so there is no distinction between 'a' and 'the' city center — context alone clarifies.

cards.spanishPhrase: Hay un parque bonito en nuestro barrio.
cards.chinesePhrase: 我们附近有一个漂亮的公园。
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒmen fùjìn yǒu yī gè piàoliang de gōngyuán.
cards.grammarNote:
附近 (fùjìn) lit. 'attached-near' — primarily an adverb or adjective meaning 'nearby' or 'in the vicinity.' Used as a noun here for neighbourhood, though 小区 (xiǎoqū) = residential community is more specific for a Chinese neighborhood complex.
我们附近 (wǒmen fùjìn) = near us / in our area.
Note this card shares the same word as 'near' and 'nearby' — context distinguishes usage.

cards.spanishPhrase: Nos encanta nadar en la playa en verano.
cards.chinesePhrase: 夏天我们喜欢在海滩游泳。
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Xiàtiān wǒmen xǐhuān zài hǎitān yóuyǒng.
cards.grammarNote:
海滩 (hǎitān) lit. 'sea-beach/sandflat' — 海 = sea/ocean, 滩 = a flat sandy or muddy stretch.
去海滩 (qù hǎitān) = go to the beach.
在海滩上 (zài hǎitān shàng) = on the beach.
A simpler variant is 沙滩 (shātān) = sandy beach (沙 = sand).
Measure word: 一片沙滩 (yī piàn shātān), using 片 for an expanse.

cards.spanishPhrase: Esa montaña es muy alta.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Nà zuò shān fēicháng gāo.
cards.grammarNote:
山 (shān) is the single-character word for mountain or hill — one of the most basic Chinese characters, pictographically depicting three peaks.
Measure word: 一座山 (yī zuò shān), using 座 for large natural formations.
爬山 (pá shān) = climb a mountain / go hiking.
山 appears widely in place names, e.g.
黄山 (Huáng Shān) = Yellow Mountain.

cards.spanishPhrase: El museo abre a las nueve.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Bówùguǎn jiǔ diǎn kāimén.
cards.grammarNote:
博物馆 (bówùguǎn) lit. 'broad-things-hall' — 博 = broad/learned, 物 = things/objects, 馆 = public hall.
参观博物馆 (cānguān bówùguǎn) = visit a museum (参观 is the correct verb for visiting cultural sites).
Measure word: 一座博物馆.
Compare 美术馆 (měishùguǎn) = art gallery/museum, specifically for fine arts.

cards.spanishPhrase: Hay un cine cerca de aquí.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Zhè fùjìn yǒu yī jiā diànyǐngyuàn.
cards.grammarNote:
电影院 (diànyǐngyuàn) lit. 'electric-shadow-theater' — one of the most evocative compound words: 电 = electricity, 影 = shadow/image, 院 = courtyard/theater.
看电影 (kàn diànyǐng) = watch a movie; 去电影院看电影 = go to the cinema to watch a film.
Measure word: 一家电影院.
Note the pattern: 院 appears in 医院, 剧院 (theater), and 电影院.

cards.spanishPhrase: ¿Tienes un mapa de la ciudad?
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Nǐ yǒu chéngshì dìtú ma?
cards.grammarNote:
地图 (dìtú) lit. 'earth/ground-diagram' — 地 = ground/earth, 图 = diagram/picture.
Measure word: 一张地图 (yī zhāng dìtú), using 张 for flat, sheet-like objects.
看地图 (kàn dìtú) = read/look at a map.
有地图吗 (yǒu dìtú ma)? = Do you have a map?
In modern usage, 导航 (dǎoháng) = GPS/navigation app is more commonly heard.

cards.spanishPhrase: ¿En qué dirección está la playa?
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Hǎitān zài nǎge fāngxiàng?
cards.grammarNote:
方向 (fāngxiàng) lit. 'square/direction-toward' — 方 = direction/side, 向 = facing toward.
Used for compass directions and abstract direction.
往那个方向走 (wǎng nà gè fāngxiàng zǒu) = walk in that direction.
Distinguish from 方面 (fāngmiàn) = aspect/side (of an issue).
The four compass points 东南西北 (dōng nán xī běi) are common compound elements in Chinese place names.

cards.spanishPhrase: ¿Cuál es la distancia al aeropuerto?
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Dào jīchǎng de jùlí shì duōshao?
cards.grammarNote:
距离 (jùlí) lit. 'apart-from / separated-by' — both characters suggest separation or distance.
到…的距离 (dào...de jùlí) = the distance to...
距离这里很近 (jùlí zhèlǐ hěn jìn) = very close from here.
In casual speech, 多远 (duō yuǎn)? = How far? is the natural question: 到机场多远? = How far is it to the airport?

cards.spanishPhrase: Me gusta viajar en verano.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ xǐhuan zài xiàtiān lǚxíng.
cards.grammarNote:
旅行 (lǚxíng) lit. 'journey-go' — 旅 = travel/journey, 行 = to go/travel.
Used as both a verb (去旅行 = go traveling) and a noun (一次旅行 = a trip).
旅游 (lǚyóu) is a close synonym, emphasizing leisure sightseeing.
Compare: 旅行 = travel broadly, 旅游 = tourism/vacation travel.
我喜欢旅行 = I like to travel.

cards.spanishPhrase: Prefiero caminar al trabajo hoy.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ jīntiān xiǎng zǒulù qù shàngbān.
cards.grammarNote:
走路 (zǒulù) lit. 'walk-road' — the verb phrase for walking as a mode of transportation.
走路去 (zǒulù qù) = go on foot / walk there.
Note that 走 alone means 'to walk' or 'to go/leave': 我走了 = I'm leaving.
路 (lù) = road/path.
Compare 散步 (sànbù) = take a leisurely stroll, which implies walking for pleasure rather than to get somewhere.

cards.spanishPhrase: Ella sabe manejar muy bien.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Tā kāichē kāi de hěn hǎo.
cards.grammarNote:
开车 (kāichē) lit. 'open/operate-vehicle' — 开 = to operate/drive, 车 = vehicle/car.
开车去 (kāichē qù) = drive there; 她开车开得很好 = she drives very well (the verb 开车 is repeated with 得 for the manner complement).
Compare 坐车 (zuò chē) = ride in a car (as a passenger).
To say 'drive to X': 开车去X.

cards.spanishPhrase: Gira a la derecha en la esquina.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Zài lùkǒu xiàng yòu zhuǎn.
cards.grammarNote:
转 (zhuǎn) = to turn or rotate.
In navigation, it pairs with direction words: 向左转 (xiàng zuǒ zhuǎn) = turn left, 向右转 (xiàng yòu zhuǎn) = turn right.
在路口转 (zài lùkǒu zhuǎn) = turn at the intersection.
A common directions phrase is 转弯 (zhuǎnwān) = make a turn.
Note the tone: 转 (zhuǎn) = to turn; 传 (chuán) = to pass/transmit — don't confuse them.

cards.spanishPhrase: Hay que cruzar la calle con cuidado.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Guò mǎlù yào xiǎoxīn.
cards.grammarNote:
穿过 (chuānguò) lit. 'pass-through' — 穿 = to pass through/pierce, 过 = through/past.
Used for crossing spaces: 穿过马路 (chuānguò mǎlù) = cross the road, 穿过公园 (chuānguò gōngyuán) = cut through the park.
The phrase card uses 过马路 (guò mǎlù) — a shorter, equally common form: 过 alone can mean to cross.
小心过马路 = cross the road carefully.

cards.spanishPhrase: Sígueme, yo conozco el camino.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Gēnzhe wǒ, wǒ zhīdào lù.
cards.grammarNote:
跟着 (gēnzhe) lit. 'follow-[continuous]' — 跟 = to follow/accompany, and 着 (zhe) marks a continuous or accompanying action.
跟着我 (gēnzhe wǒ) = follow me.
Note the structure: 着 attaches to verbs to indicate ongoing action.
Compare 跟 used as a preposition: 跟我来 (gēn wǒ lái) = come with me.
按照 (ànzhào) is used for 'follow' in the sense of following instructions.

cards.spanishPhrase: Tardamos dos horas en llegar al hotel.
cards.chinesePhrase: 我们花了两小时到达酒店。
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒmen huāle liǎng xiǎoshí dàodá jiǔdiàn.
cards.grammarNote:
到达 (dàodá) lit. 'arrive-reach' — a slightly formal compound where both characters mean arrival.
Used in written and spoken contexts: 到达机场 (dàodá jīchǎng) = arrive at the airport.
In casual speech, simply 到 (dào) suffices: 我们到了 (wǒmen dào le) = we've arrived.
到达 is more formal and common in announcements, schedules, and written directions.

cards.spanishPhrase: Por favor, entra por la puerta principal.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Qǐng cóng zhèngmén jìnrù.
cards.grammarNote:
进入 (jìnrù) lit. 'enter-go-into' — a slightly formal compound; the casual verb is simply 进 (jìn).
进来 (jìn lái) = come in, 进去 (jìn qù) = go in.
从正门进入 (cóng zhèngmén jìnrù) = enter through the main entrance — 从 (cóng) = from/through.
Note: Chinese direction complements 来/去 indicate movement toward or away from the speaker.

cards.spanishPhrase: Tenemos que salir antes de las ocho.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒmen bìxū bā diǎn qián líkāi.
cards.grammarNote:
离开 (líkāi) lit. 'separate-open' — 离 = to part from/away from, 开 adds the sense of movement away.
离开这里 (líkāi zhèlǐ) = leave here; 必须离开 (bìxū líkāi) = must leave.
Compare 出发 (chūfā) = depart (set off on a journey) and 走 (zǒu) = leave/go (casual).
离开 always implies leaving a person or place behind.

cards.spanishPhrase: El tren parte a las tres.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Huǒchē sān diǎn chūfā.
cards.grammarNote:
出发 (chūfā) lit. 'go-out-set-off' — 出 = to go out, 发 = to start/send out.
Used for the beginning of a trip or journey: 我们出发吧 (wǒmen chūfā ba) = let's set off!
几点出发 (jǐ diǎn chūfā)? = What time do we depart?
Contrast with 离开 (líkāi) = leave (depart from a specific place) — 出发 focuses on the start of a journey toward a destination.

cards.spanishPhrase: Quiero visitar el museo mañana.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ míngtiān xiǎng cānguān bówùguǎn.
cards.grammarNote:
参观 (cānguān) lit. 'participate-observe' — the correct verb for visiting places like museums, schools, factories, and attractions.
It implies a structured or purposeful visit.
参观博物馆 (cānguān bówùguǎn) = visit the museum.
Don't use 参观 for visiting people — use 拜访 (bàifǎng) for formal visits or 看望 (kànwàng) for visiting friends and family.

cards.spanishPhrase: El gato está en la caja.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Māo zài hézi lǐ.
cards.grammarNote:
在…里 (zài...lǐ) = inside something — 在 marks location, 里 = inside/interior.
在盒子里 (zài hézi lǐ) = inside the box.
里 is often optional when the container is obvious: 在房间 (zài fángjiān) = in the room is understood without 里.
Compare 在…中 (zài...zhōng) = in the middle of / among, used for more abstract containment: 在生活中 = in life.

cards.spanishPhrase: El libro está sobre la mesa.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Shū zài zhuōzi shàng.
cards.grammarNote:
在…上 (zài...shàng) = on top of something — 在 marks location, 上 = above/on top.
书在桌子上 (shū zài zhuōzi shàng) = the book is on the table.
上 also indicates surface contact: 在墙上 = on the wall.
In Chinese, location words like 上, 下, 里, 旁边 always follow the noun they modify: 桌子上 (on the table), never 上桌子.

cards.spanishPhrase: Te espero en la entrada.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ zài ménkǒu děng nǐ.
cards.grammarNote:
在 (zài) alone means 'at' or 'in' a location — it is the core location verb and preposition in Chinese.
Subject + 在 + place: 我在家 (wǒ zài jiā) = I'm at home.
在 can also mark ongoing action: 他在看书 (tā zài kàn shū) = he is reading.
Unlike Spanish 'en,' 在 is a full verb meaning 'to be at/in': 你在哪儿 (nǐ zài nǎr)? = Where are you?

cards.spanishPhrase: Las llaves están debajo de la silla.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Yàoshi zài yǐzi xiàmiàn.
cards.grammarNote:
在…下面 (zài...xiàmiàn) = under/below something — 下面 = the underside/below.
钥匙在椅子下面 (yàoshi zài yǐzi xiàmiàn) = the keys are under the chair.
You can shorten to 在…下 (zài...xià) in everyday speech.
The pattern holds for all positional words: 下面 (below), 上面 (above), 里面 (inside), 外面 (outside) — the noun comes before the position word.

cards.spanishPhrase: El banco está al lado del hospital.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Yínháng zài yīyuàn pángbiān.
cards.grammarNote:
旁边 (pángbiān) lit. 'side-side' — both characters convey being beside something.
在…旁边 (zài...pángbiān) = next to / beside: 银行在医院旁边 = the bank is next to the hospital.
This follows the standard Chinese location pattern: 在 + reference noun + position word.
Compare 附近 (fùjìn) = nearby (a broader area) vs.
旁边 = directly beside.

cards.spanishPhrase: La tienda está entre dos cafeterías.
cards.chinesePhrase: 商店在两家咖啡馆之间。
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Shāngdiàn zài liǎng jiā kāfēiguǎn zhījiān.
cards.grammarNote:
之间 (zhījiān) lit. 'of-between' — 之 is a classical linking particle, 间 = space between.
Used after a noun phrase: 两家咖啡馆之间 (liǎng jiā kāfēiguǎn zhījiān) = between two cafés.
A simpler spoken form is 中间 (zhōngjiān) = in the middle / between: 在学校和公园中间 = between the school and the park.
之间 is slightly more formal or written.

cards.spanishPhrase: Nos vemos en frente de la escuela.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒmen zài xuéxiào qiánmiàn jiànmiàn.
cards.grammarNote:
前面 (qiánmiàn) lit. 'front-side' — 前 = front/before, 面 = surface/side.
在…前面 (zài...qiánmiàn) = in front of: 在学校前面 = in front of the school.
前面 also means 'ahead' in directions: 前面有个路口 = there's an intersection ahead.
The opposite is 后面 (hòumiàn) = behind.
Note that 面 is a suffix making the direction word into a location noun.

cards.spanishPhrase: El coche está detrás del edificio.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Qìchē zài lóu hòumiàn.
cards.grammarNote:
后面 (hòumiàn) lit. 'rear-side' — the opposite of 前面.
在楼后面 (zài lóu hòumiàn) = behind the building.
后 also means 'after' in time: 以后 (yǐhòu) = after/in the future, 然后 (ránhòu) = then/afterwards.
In directions: 后面 can mean 'further back' or 'behind you.' The full pattern is 在 + reference + 后面.

cards.spanishPhrase: Vivo cerca del parque.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ zhù zài gōngyuán fùjìn.
cards.grammarNote:
附近 (fùjìn) lit. 'attached-near' — works as an adjective or adverb placed after the reference noun: 学校附近 (xuéxiào fùjìn) = near the school.
附近有…吗 (fùjìn yǒu...ma)? = Is there a ... nearby?
Note that 附近 follows the reference noun, unlike English 'near': 学校附近 = near the school (lit. 'school-vicinity').

cards.spanishPhrase: Hay flores en todas partes del jardín.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Huāyuán lǐ dàochù dōu shì huā.
cards.grammarNote:
到处 (dàochù) lit. 'arrive-everywhere' — 到 = to reach/everywhere, 处 = place.
到处都是 (dàochù dōu shì) = everywhere there is/are: 到处都是花 = there are flowers everywhere.
The word 都 (dōu) = all/both often pairs with 到处 to reinforce universality.
Compare 各处 (gè chù) = various places and 处处 (chùchù) = everywhere (more literary).

cards.spanishPhrase: Quiero ir en algún lugar tranquilo.
cards.chinesePhrase: 我想去某处安静的地方。
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ xiǎng qù mǒu chù ānjìng de dìfang.
cards.grammarNote:
某处 (mǒu chù) lit. 'some-place' — 某 = a certain/some (unspecified), 处 = place.
Used for an unspecified or unknown location: 某处安静的地方 = some quiet place.
More colloquial alternatives: 某个地方 (mǒu gè dìfāng) = somewhere, some place.
In questions, use 哪里 (nǎlǐ) or 哪儿 (nǎr) = where: 你在哪里? = Where are you?

cards.spanishPhrase: No tengo en ningún lugar adonde ir.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ nǎr dōu bù xiǎng qù.
cards.grammarNote:
哪儿都不 (nǎr dōu bù) lit. 'where-all-not' — Chinese expresses 'nowhere' by combining the question word 哪儿/哪里 (where) with 都 (all) and a negative: 哪儿都不想去 = don't want to go anywhere / nowhere to go.
This double-scope negation is natural in Chinese.
Alternatively: 没有地方 (méiyǒu dìfāng) = no place.
Note: 都不 = none of them; 都没 = have none.

cards.spanishPhrase: Gira a la izquierda en el semáforo.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Zài hónglǜdēng chù xiàng zuǒbiān zhuǎn.
cards.grammarNote:
左边 (zuǒbiān) lit. 'left-side' — 左 = left, 边 = side/edge.
Used for both abstract direction and physical location.
在左边 (zài zuǒbiān) = on the left; 向左转 (xiàng zuǒ zhuǎn) = turn left.
The shorter form 左 alone is used in commands: 左转!
The opposite is 右边 (yòubiān) = right side.
左右 together means 'approximately' (left-right = around): 两点左右 = around 2 o'clock.

cards.spanishPhrase: La farmacia está a la derecha.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Yàodiàn zài yòubiān.
cards.grammarNote:
右边 (yòubiān) lit. 'right-side' — the mirror pair of 左边.
在右边 (zài yòubiān) = on the right; 向右转 (xiàng yòu zhuǎn) = turn right.
In Chinese, right (右) is not associated with 'correct' the way it is in English — 对 (duì) means correct.
A handy mnemonic: 右 has a mouth (口) on the right side of the character, 左 has a hand radical on the left.

cards.spanishPhrase: Sigue derecho por esta calle.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Yánzhe zhè tiáo jiē zhí zǒu.
cards.grammarNote:
直走 (zhí zǒu) lit. 'straight-walk' — the standard direction command for 'go straight.' 沿着这条街直走 (yánzhe zhè tiáo jiē zhí zǒu) = go straight along this street; 沿着 = along.
A fuller form is 一直走 (yīzhí zǒu), where 一直 = continuously/straight: 一直走到路口 = keep going straight to the intersection.
直 also means 'vertical' or 'direct' in other contexts.

cards.spanishPhrase: El lago está al norte de la ciudad.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Hú zài chéngshì de běifāng.
cards.grammarNote:
北方 (běifāng) lit. 'north-direction' — 方 turns the compass character into a direction noun.
For street addresses and navigation, the shorter 北 (běi) is used: 向北走 = go north.
The four cardinal directions in Chinese are 东南西北 (dōng nán xī běi) — a fixed order worth memorizing.
北方 also culturally refers to northern China as a region, in contrast to 南方 (nánfāng) = the south.

cards.spanishPhrase: El clima es cálido en el sur.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Nánfāng de tiānqì hěn nuǎnhuo.
cards.grammarNote:
南方 (nánfāng) lit. 'south-direction' — culturally, 南方 in China refers to the regions south of the Yangtze River, known for warmer, wetter climates and rice-based cuisine.
向南走 (xiàng nán zǒu) = walk south.
南 appears in place names: 海南 (Hǎinán) = Hainan (south sea), 南京 (Nánjīng) = Nanjing (southern capital).
Contrast with 北方 = north.

cards.spanishPhrase: El sol sale por el este.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Tàiyáng cóng dōngfāng shēngqǐ.
cards.grammarNote:
东方 (dōngfāng) lit. 'east-direction' — 东 is east and also culturally means 'the East/Orient': 东方 is the classical Chinese word for 'the East' as a concept.
太阳从东方升起 = the sun rises in the east.
In everyday navigation: 向东走 (xiàng dōng zǒu) = go east.
东 features in 东京 (Dōngjīng) = Tokyo (eastern capital) and 东南 (dōngnán) = southeast.

cards.spanishPhrase: El sol se pone por el oeste.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Tàiyáng cóng xīfāng luòxià.
cards.grammarNote:
西方 (xīfāng) lit. 'west-direction' — 西方 is also the classical Chinese term for 'the West' (i.e., Western countries and cultures).
太阳从西方落下 = the sun sets in the west.
In directions: 向西走 (xiàng xī zǒu) = go west.
西 appears in 西班牙 (Xībānyá) = Spain, 西安 (Xī'ān) = Xi'an (western peace), and 东西 (dōngxi) = things/stuff (lit. east-west).

cards.spanishPhrase: Ella vive en una ciudad grande.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Tā zhù zài yīgè dà chéngshì.
cards.grammarNote:
住 (zhù) = to live (reside) or to stay.
住在 + place: 住在北京 (zhù zài Běijīng) = live in Beijing.
Don't confuse with 生活 (shēnghuó) = to live (as in 'to be alive' or 'to live one's life').
住 specifically means to reside at an address.
你住在哪里 (nǐ zhù zài nǎlǐ)? = Where do you live?
A useful near-synonym: 居住 (jūzhù) = reside (more formal).

cards.spanishPhrase: Me voy a quedar aquí esta noche.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ jīn wǎn yào dāi zài zhèlǐ.
cards.grammarNote:
待 (dāi) = to stay or remain somewhere — used for temporary presence.
待在家里 (dāi zài jiāli) = stay at home.
Often paired with time: 待一会儿 (dāi yīhuìr) = stay a while.
Don't confuse with 住 (zhù) = to live/reside (more permanent).
Another common form is 呆 (dāi), a variant character with the same pronunciation and meaning used interchangeably in informal writing.

cards.spanishPhrase: Por favor espera aquí un momento.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Qǐng zài zhèlǐ děng yīxià.
cards.grammarNote:
等 (děng) = to wait or to wait for.
等一下 (děng yīxià) = wait a moment — very common in daily speech.
等你 (děng nǐ) = wait for you.
在这里等 (zài zhèlǐ děng) = wait here.
Note that 等 directly takes a person or thing as its object: 等公交车 (děng gōngjiāochē) = wait for the bus.
等 also means 'etc.' in lists: 苹果、香蕉等 = apples, bananas, etc.

cards.spanishPhrase: ¿Dónde podemos encontrarnos mañana?
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒmen míngtiān zài nǎlǐ jiànmiàn?
cards.grammarNote:
见面 (jiànmiàn) lit. 'see-face' — a verb-object compound meaning to meet (in person).
Because 面 is already the object, you cannot add another object: say 和他见面 (hé tā jiànmiàn) = meet with him, not ~~见面他~~.
我们在哪里见面 = where shall we meet?
Compare 认识 (rènshi) = to meet for the first time / to get to know someone; 见面 is used for arranged meetings.

cards.spanishPhrase: Me perdí en el centro de la ciudad.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ zài shì zhōngxīn mílù le.
cards.grammarNote:
迷路 (mílù) lit. 'confused-road' — 迷 = to be confused/lost, 路 = road.
迷路了 (mílù le) = (I/someone) got lost — 了 marks the change of state.
我迷路了 is the go-to phrase when lost.
Compare 找不到路 (zhǎo bù dào lù) = can't find the way.
迷 also appears in 着迷 (zháomí) = to be fascinated by, and 迷 fans (球迷 = sports fan, 歌迷 = music fan).

cards.spanishPhrase: Por fin encontré mis llaves.
cards.chinesePhrase: 我终于找到我的钥匙了。
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ zhōngyú zhǎodào wǒ de yàoshi le.
cards.grammarNote:
找到 (zhǎodào) = to find (successfully) — 找 = to look for, and the resultative complement 到 (dào) indicates the search succeeded.
Without 到: 我在找 (wǒ zài zhǎo) = I am looking for (still searching).
With 到: 我找到了 (wǒ zhǎodào le) = I found it.
This 找/找到 pair illustrates a key Chinese pattern: verb alone = attempt, verb + resultative complement = outcome.

cards.spanishPhrase: Estoy buscando una farmacia cercana.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ zài zhǎo fùjìn de yàodiàn.
cards.grammarNote:
找 (zhǎo) = to look for or to seek — without a result complement, it means the search is ongoing.
我在找 (wǒ zài zhǎo) = I am looking for...
找附近的药店 = look for a nearby pharmacy.
Compare 找到 (zhǎodào) = to have found (success).
找 also means to look someone up: 我去找他 (wǒ qù zhǎo tā) = I'm going to find/see him.
A synonym for searching: 寻找 (xúnzhǎo) — more formal.

cards.spanishPhrase: Nos dirigimos al aeropuerto ahora.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒmen xiànzài qiánwǎng jīchǎng.
cards.grammarNote:
前往 (qiánwǎng) lit. 'forward-go' — a formal or written expression for heading to a destination.
前往机场 (qiánwǎng jīchǎng) = head to the airport.
In casual speech, 去 (qù) is sufficient: 我们去机场 = we're going to the airport.
前往 is common in announcements, flight information boards, and formal instructions.
前 = forward/ahead; 往 = toward.

cards.spanishPhrase: Continúa por este camino recto.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Yánzhe zhè tiáo lù jìxù zǒu.
cards.grammarNote:
继续 (jìxù) lit. 'continue-follow' — both characters convey continuation.
Works as a verb before another verb: 继续走 (jìxù zǒu) = keep walking, 继续看 (jìxù kàn) = keep watching.
继续 + [action] is the standard structure; no grammatical particle is needed between them.
Common phrase: 请继续 (qǐng jìxù) = please continue.
Synonym: 一直 (yīzhí) = keep on / straight (for ongoing physical movement).

cards.spanishPhrase: ¡Para aquí, por favor!
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Qǐng zài zhèlǐ tíng xià!
cards.grammarNote:
停下 (tíng xià) lit. 'stop-down' — 停 = to stop, 下 = a directional complement indicating the action is completed/settled.
请停下 (qǐng tíng xià) = please stop.
停 alone also works: 停! = Stop!
For vehicles stopping, 停车 (tíngchē) is used.
停下来 (tíng xià lái) adds 来, emphasizing the motion toward the speaker or a settled state: very common in commands.

cards.spanishPhrase: ¿Dónde puedo estacionar el coche?
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ kěyǐ zài nǎlǐ tíngchē?
cards.grammarNote:
停车 (tíngchē) lit. 'stop-vehicle' — 停 = to stop, 车 = vehicle.
The standard term for parking a car.
停车场 (tíngchēchǎng) = parking lot (lit. 'stop-vehicle-ground').
停车位 (tíngchēwèi) = parking space.
可以在这里停车吗 (kěyǐ zài zhèlǐ tíngchē ma)? = Can I park here?
不准停车 (bùzhǔn tíngchē) = No parking — a common sign.

cards.spanishPhrase: Quiero reservar una habitación para dos.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ xiǎng yùdìng yī jiān shuāngrén fáng.
cards.grammarNote:
预订 (yùdìng) lit. 'in-advance-settle/order' — 预 = beforehand, 订 = to order/book.
Used for reserving hotels, restaurants, tickets, and services.
预订一间双人房 (yùdìng yī jiān shuāngrénfáng) = book a double room — note 间 (jiān) as the measure word for rooms.
A variant 预定 (yùdìng, same pronunciation) is interchangeable in most contexts.
订 alone = to order/subscribe.

cards.spanishPhrase: ¿A qué hora puedo registrarme?
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ jǐ diǎn kěyǐ bànlǐ rùzhù?
cards.grammarNote:
办理入住 (bànlǐ rùzhù) lit. 'handle-formalities for entering-stay' — 办理 = to handle/process, 入住 = to check in/move in.
This is the formal hotel phrase.
In casual usage, people often just say 入住 or 登记 (dēngjì) = register.
几点可以办理入住 (jǐ diǎn kěyǐ bànlǐ rùzhù)? = What time can I check in?
At airports, 登机 (dēngjī) = board a plane, while 值机 (zhíjī) = flight check-in.

cards.spanishPhrase: Necesito hacer el check-out mañana temprano.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ míngtiān zǎoshang xūyào tuìfáng.
cards.grammarNote:
退房 (tuìfáng) lit. 'return/give-back-room' — 退 = to return or give back, 房 = room.
The standard hotel check-out phrase.
我要退房 (wǒ yào tuìfáng) = I want to check out.
退房时间 (tuìfáng shíjiān) = check-out time.
Compare 入住 (rùzhù) = check in (enter-stay).
退 appears in other return contexts: 退票 (tuìpiào) = refund a ticket, 退款 (tuìkuǎn) = refund money.

cards.spanishPhrase: ¿Hay un restaurante cerca?
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Fùjìn yǒu cāntīng ma?
cards.grammarNote:
附近 (fùjìn) lit. 'attached-near' — functions as an adjective or adverb placed after the reference noun: 学校附近 (xuéxiào fùjìn) = near the school.
As a standalone adverb: 附近有餐厅吗 (fùjìn yǒu cāntīng ma)? = Is there a restaurant nearby?
Note this same word appears in the cards for 'neighbourhood' and 'near' — Chinese is flexible, and context clarifies meaning.
No article is used in Chinese.

cards.spanishPhrase: Este barrio es muy tranquilo por la noche.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Zhège jiēqū wǎnshang hěn ānjìng.
cards.grammarNote:
安静 (ānjìng) lit. 'peaceful-still' — 安 = peaceful/safe, 静 = still/quiet.
Used as an adjective (这里很安静 = it's very quiet here) or a command (安静! = Quiet! / Be quiet!).
The 的 (de) in 安静的 links it to the noun it modifies: 安静的地方 = a quiet place.
Compare 安全 (ānquán) = safe (安 + 全 = whole).
安 is a reassuring character appearing in many positive words.

cards.spanishPhrase: Estoy perdido, ¿me puedes ayudar?
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ mílù le, nǐ néng bāng wǒ ma?
cards.grammarNote:
迷路了 (mílù le) = (I am) lost — 迷路 is the verb 'to get lost,' and 了 marks the completed change of state (now in the condition of being lost).
我迷路了 (wǒ mílù le) is the natural distress phrase: I'm lost.
To ask for help: 你能帮我吗 (nǐ néng bāng wǒ ma)? = Can you help me?
Compare the verb card 迷路 — same word; here the 了 makes it an adjective-like state.

cards.spanishPhrase: El médico me recetó una medicina.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Yīshēng gěi wǒ kāile yào.
cards.grammarNote:
医生 (yīshēng) is built from 医 (yī, medicine/heal) + 生 (shēng, person/born) — literally 'medicine person.' The 生 suffix appears in many professional titles: 学生 (xuésheng, student), 先生 (xiānsheng, mister/teacher).
Count doctors with the measure word 位 (wèi) for respected persons: 一位医生 (yī wèi yīshēng).
In the example phrase 医生给我开了药 (the doctor prescribed medicine for me), 开药 (kāi yào, 'open medicine') means 'to prescribe.'

cards.spanishPhrase: La enfermera es muy amable.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Nà wèi hùshi fēicháng yǒuhǎo.
cards.grammarNote:
护士 (hùshi) combines 护 (hù, to protect/guard) + 士 (shì, a person of skill or status) — 'one who guards/protects.' The 士 suffix also appears in 博士 (bóshì, PhD) and 律师 (lǜshī).
Use 位 (wèi) as the measure word: 一位护士.
Note the neutral tone on 士 in speech: hùshi, not hùshì.
The phrase 那位护士非常友好 uses 那位 (nà wèi) as a respectful demonstrative.

cards.spanishPhrase: Mi hermano trabaja como ingeniero.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ gēgē shì yī míng gōngchéngshī.
cards.grammarNote:
工程师 (gōngchéngshī) breaks down as 工程 (gōngchéng, engineering/project) + 师 (shī, master/expert).
The 师 suffix marks expertise: 老师 (lǎoshī, teacher), 厨师 (chúshī, chef), 建筑师 (jiànzhùshī, architect).
工程 itself means 'a project' in modern usage: 这个工程很大 (this project is very large).
Count with 名 (míng) or 位 (wèi): 一名工程师.

cards.spanishPhrase: Ella es programadora en una empresa grande.
cards.chinesePhrase: 她是一家大公司的程序员。
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Tā shì yī jiā dà gōngsī de chéngxùyuán.
cards.grammarNote:
程序员 (chéngxùyuán) is 程序 (chéngxù, program/procedure) + 员 (yuán, person/member).
The 员 suffix is very productive for occupational roles: 服务员 (fúwùyuán, waiter), 售票员 (shòupiàoyuán, ticket seller), 演员 (yǎnyuán, actor).
程序 also means 'procedure' in formal contexts (e.g., legal or bureaucratic steps).
A colloquial term is 码农 (mǎnóng, 'code farmer'), a humorous self-deprecating label popular among developers.

cards.spanishPhrase: El cocinero prepara comida deliciosa.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Chúshī zuò de cài hěn hǎochī.
cards.grammarNote:
厨师 (chúshī) is 厨 (chú, kitchen) + 师 (shī, expert) — 'kitchen expert.' The character 厨 includes the 广 (shelter) radical, associating it with a covered space.
Distinguish 厨师 (professional cook) from 做饭 (zuòfàn, to cook at home).
The phrase 厨师做的菜很好吃 uses the 做的 (zuò de) construction to modify a noun: 'dishes that the cook made.' Count with 位 (wèi): 一位厨师.

cards.spanishPhrase: El camarero nos trajo el menú.
cards.chinesePhrase: 服务员给我们拿来了菜单。
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Fúwùyuán gěi wǒmen ná lái le càidān.
cards.grammarNote:
服务员 (fúwùyuán) literally means 'service person' — 服务 (fúwù, service) + 员 (yuán, person).
It is the standard term for restaurant servers, shop assistants, and hotel staff in China.
To call a server, say 服务员！ directly — this is not rude in Chinese culture.
The phrase 服务员给我们拿来了菜单 uses 拿来 (nálái, to bring over), a directional complement meaning 'bring here.'

cards.spanishPhrase: Habla con el gerente sobre el problema.
cards.chinesePhrase: 关于这个问题请找经理谈。
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Guānyú zhège wèntí qǐng zhǎo jīnglǐ tán.
cards.grammarNote:
经理 (jīnglǐ) combines 经 (jīng, to manage/pass through) + 理 (lǐ, to put in order/reason).
Together they mean 'one who manages affairs.' The word 经 appears in 经济 (jīngjì, economy) and 经验 (jīngyàn, experience).
Titles like 总经理 (zǒngjīnglǐ, general manager) or 副经理 (fù jīnglǐ, deputy manager) are built on this base.
Use 位 (wèi) as measure: 一位经理.

cards.spanishPhrase: Soy estudiante de español.
cards.chinesePhrase: 我是学西班牙语的学生。
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ shì xué Xībānyáyǔ de xuésheng.
cards.grammarNote:
学生 (xuésheng) is 学 (xué, to study/learn) + 生 (shēng, person).
The second syllable is toneless in natural speech: xuésheng, not xuéshēng.
Compare 学生 (student) with 学习 (xuéxí, to study) and 学校 (xuéxiào, school) — all share the 学 root.
The measure word is 个 (gè) or 名 (míng): 一个学生, 一名学生.
学生 can refer to students at any level, from primary school through university.

cards.spanishPhrase: El arquitecto diseñó ese edificio moderno.
cards.chinesePhrase: 建筑师设计了那栋现代楼。
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Jiànzhùshī shèjì le nà dòng xiàndài lóu.
cards.grammarNote:
建筑师 (jiànzhùshī) is 建筑 (jiànzhù, architecture/construction) + 师 (shī, expert).
建 (jiàn, to build/establish) also appears in 建设 (jiànshè, to build up/develop) and 建议 (jiànyì, to suggest).
筑 (zhù) specifically means to construct with materials.
The phrase 建筑师设计了那栋现代楼 uses 栋 (dòng) as the measure word for buildings — a key measure word to learn: 一栋楼 (one building).

cards.spanishPhrase: Necesito hablar con un abogado.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ xūyào hé lǜshī tán tan.
cards.grammarNote:
律师 (lǜshī) is 律 (lǜ, law/statute) + 师 (shī, expert) — 'law expert.' The character 律 appears in 纪律 (jìlǜ, discipline), 法律 (fǎlǜ, law/legislation), and 律动 (lǜdòng, rhythm).
Compare with 法官 (fǎguān, judge) and 检察官 (jiǎncháguān, prosecutor).
The phrase 我需要和律师谈谈 uses 谈谈 (tán tán) — reduplication of the verb 谈 to soften the meaning to 'have a chat/talk.'

cards.spanishPhrase: Mi abuelo es un jubilado feliz.
cards.chinesePhrase: 我爷爷是一位快乐的退休人员。
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ yéye shì yī wèi kuàilè de tuìxiū rényuán.
cards.grammarNote:
退休人员 (tuìxiū rényuán) is the formal compound for 'retired person' — 退休 (tuìxiū, to retire) + 人员 (rényuán, personnel/person).
In casual speech, 退休人员 is often shortened to 退休的人 or simply 退休了 (retired).
退 (tuì) means 'to step back/withdraw' and appears in 退出 (tuìchū, to withdraw) and 退款 (tuìkuǎn, refund).
Measure: 位 (wèi) for respectful reference.

cards.spanishPhrase: Me gusta leer antes de dormir.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ xǐhuan shuì qián yuèdú.
cards.grammarNote:
阅读 (yuèdú) is a formal two-character compound for reading, used for books, documents, and sustained reading.
Compare with the more casual 看书 (kànshū, to read/look at a book) or 看报 (kànbào, to read the newspaper).
阅 (yuè) carries the sense of reviewing or inspecting text; 读 (dú) means to read aloud or study.
The phrase 睡前阅读 (shuì qián yuèdú) means 'reading before sleep' — 睡前 is a common time expression meaning 'before sleeping.'

cards.spanishPhrase: Ella disfruta escribir cuentos cortos.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Tā xǐhuan xiězuò duǎnpiān gùshi.
cards.grammarNote:
写作 (xiězuò) means writing as a creative or compositional activity — 写 (xiě, to write) + 作 (zuò, to make/create).
It refers to creative writing, authorship, or composition, not everyday writing tasks.
For casual writing, use 写字 (xiězì, write characters) or 写信 (xiě xìn, write a letter).
短篇故事 (duǎnpiān gùshi, short story) in the example uses 短篇 (duǎnpiān, short piece) as a modifier — a useful pattern: 长篇小说 (long novel), 短篇小说 (short story).

cards.spanishPhrase: Le encanta pintar paisajes naturales.
cards.chinesePhrase: 他非常喜欢绘画自然风景。
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Tā fēicháng xǐhuan huìhuà zìrán fēngjǐng.
cards.grammarNote:
绘画 (huìhuà) refers to painting and visual art broadly — 绘 (huì, to draw/paint) + 画 (huà, painting/picture).
Both characters share the meaning of making visual images, making this a synonym compound.
In everyday speech, 画画 (huàhuà, 'paint-paint') is more common and casual: 她喜欢画画.
A painting as an object uses the measure word 幅 (fú): 一幅画 (one painting).
The verb 欣赏 (xīnshǎng, to appreciate) often pairs with art: 欣赏绘画.

cards.spanishPhrase: Él sabe dibujar muy bien.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Tā de sùmiáo huà de fēicháng hǎo.
cards.grammarNote:
素描 (sùmiáo) specifically means sketching or drawing in pencil/charcoal — 素 (sù, plain/simple/unadorned) + 描 (miáo, to trace/depict).
It is a loanword concept from fine arts.
For general 'drawing,' Chinese also uses 画 (huà) or 画画 (huàhuà).
The phrase 素描画得非常好 uses 画得 + adjective, the result complement pattern: 画得好 means 'draws well.' To say someone draws badly: 画得不好.

cards.spanishPhrase: La fotografía es su pasatiempo favorito.
cards.chinesePhrase: 摄影是她最喜欢的爱好。
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Shèyǐng shì tā zuì xǐhuan de àihào.
cards.grammarNote:
摄影 (shèyǐng) is 摄 (shè, to capture/absorb) + 影 (yǐng, shadow/image) — literally 'to capture shadows/images,' a fitting description of photography.
影 also appears in 电影 (diànyǐng, movie) and 影响 (yǐngxiǎng, influence).
A photographer is 摄影师 (shèyǐngshī).
To take a photo casually, use 拍照 (pāizhào): 我想拍照.
摄影 implies photography as an art or profession, while 拍照 is the everyday act of taking pictures.

cards.spanishPhrase: La música me hace sentir feliz.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Yīnyuè ràng wǒ gǎndào kuàilè.
cards.grammarNote:
音乐 (yīnyuè) is 音 (yīn, sound/tone) + 乐 (yuè, music).
Note that 乐 is pronounced yuè (music) here, not lè (happy) — the same character has two readings depending on context: 快乐 (kuàilè, happy) vs 音乐 (yīnyuè, music).
The phrase 音乐让我感到快乐 uses the causative 让 (ràng, to make/let): [subject] 让 [object] [verb/adjective].
This is an extremely common construction: 这让我很开心 (this makes me happy).

cards.spanishPhrase: A los niños les encanta cantar juntos.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Háizimen xǐhuan yīqǐ chànggē.
cards.grammarNote:
唱歌 (chànggē) is a verb-object compound: 唱 (chàng, to sing) + 歌 (gē, song).
Like many verb-object compounds in Chinese, they can be split: 唱了一首歌 (sang a song), 唱什么歌 (what song to sing).
The measure word for songs is 首 (shǒu): 一首歌.
The phrase 孩子们喜欢一起唱歌 uses 一起 (yīqǐ, together) — place it before the verb: 一起学习, 一起吃饭, 一起跑步.

cards.spanishPhrase: Ellos bailan juntos los fines de semana.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Tāmen zhōumò yīqǐ tiàowǔ.
cards.grammarNote:
跳舞 (tiàowǔ) is 跳 (tiào, to jump/leap) + 舞 (wǔ, dance) — 'to jump-dance.' The 跳 character appears in many movement verbs: 跳高 (tiàogāo, high jump), 跳绳 (tiàoshéng, jump rope).
跳舞 is also a verb-object compound and can be split: 跳了一支舞 (danced a dance), where 支 (zhī) is the measure word for dances or songs.
The phrase 他们周末一起跳舞 is a clean example of time-place-action word order.

cards.spanishPhrase: Cocinar en casa es más barato.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Zài jiā pēngrèn bǐjiào piányí.
cards.grammarNote:
烹饪 (pēngrèn) is the formal compound for cooking as a craft: 烹 (pēng, to cook by boiling) + 饪 (rèn, to cook until done).
In everyday speech, 做饭 (zuòfàn, make rice/cook) is far more common: 你会做饭吗?
烹饪 tends to appear in recipes, culinary programs, or when discussing cooking as a skill.
The phrase 在家烹饪比较便宜 uses 比较 (bǐjiào, comparatively/relatively) to soften a comparison: 'cooking at home is relatively cheaper.'

cards.spanishPhrase: La jardinería me relaja mucho.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Yuányì ràng wǒ hěn fàngsōng.
cards.grammarNote:
园艺 (yuányì) is 园 (yuán, garden/park) + 艺 (yì, art/skill) — 'the art of the garden.' 园 appears in 公园 (gōngyuán, park), 果园 (guǒyuán, orchard), and 动物园 (dòngwùyuán, zoo).
艺 appears in 艺术 (yìshù, art) and 手艺 (shǒuyì, craftsmanship).
The phrase 园艺让我很放松 uses the same causative 让 pattern: gardening makes me very relaxed.
放松 (fàngsōng) literally means 'to release-loosen.'

cards.spanishPhrase: El senderismo es bueno para la salud.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Túbù lǚxíng duì jiànkāng hěn hǎo.
cards.grammarNote:
徒步旅行 (túbù lǚxíng) literally means 'on-foot travel' — 徒步 (túbù, on foot) + 旅行 (lǚxíng, travel).
The shorter form 爬山 (páshān, mountain climbing) is common for hiking in a mountainous setting.
徒 (tú) means 'on foot' or 'empty-handed,' appearing in 徒弟 (túdì, apprentice) and 徒手 (túshǒu, bare-handed).
The phrase 对健康很好 (hǎo for health) is a useful template: 对…很好 (good for…): 对皮肤很好, 对身体很好.

cards.spanishPhrase: Corro todos los días por la mañana.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ měitiān zǎoshang pǎobù.
cards.grammarNote:
跑步 (pǎobù) is 跑 (pǎo, to run) + 步 (bù, step/pace) — literally 'to run steps.' 步 appears in 步行 (bùxíng, to walk), 散步 (sànbù, to take a stroll), and 进步 (jìnbù, progress).
The phrase 我每天早上跑步 demonstrates the typical Chinese time adverb order: subject + frequency (每天) + time of day (早上) + activity.
This order — general to specific — applies broadly: 每周一早上开会.

cards.spanishPhrase: Trotar media hora es muy saludable.
cards.chinesePhrase: 慢跑半小时对身体很好。
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Màn pǎo bàn xiǎoshí duì shēntǐ hěn hǎo.
cards.grammarNote:
慢跑 (màn pǎo) is simply 慢 (màn, slow) + 跑 (pǎo, to run) — 'slow running.' Chinese readily forms compound activity words by prefixing a manner adverb: 慢走 (walk slowly), 快跑 (run fast).
Compare 慢跑 (jogging) with 跑步 (running) — the distinction mirrors English.
The phrase 慢跑半小时 means 'jog for half an hour' — 半小时 (bàn xiǎoshí, half hour).
Note: duration goes after the verb without a preposition: 跑步二十分钟.

cards.spanishPhrase: Voy al trabajo en bicicleta cada día.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ měitiān qí zìxíngchē shàngbān.
cards.grammarNote:
骑自行车 (qí zìxíngchē) is 骑 (qí, to ride astride) + 自行车 (zìxíngchē, bicycle — literally 'self-propelling vehicle').
骑 is used for riding things you straddle: horses (骑马), motorcycles (骑摩托车), bicycles.
Compare with 开 (kāi, to drive) for cars: 开车.
A shorter colloquial form is 骑车 (qí chē).
The phrase 每天骑自行车上班 uses the common 上班 (shàngbān, go to work) pattern: 骑车上班, 开车上班, 走路上班.

cards.spanishPhrase: Los niños aprenden a nadar en verano.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Háizimen xiàtiān xué yóuyǒng.
cards.grammarNote:
游泳 (yóuyǒng) is 游 (yóu, to swim/travel/roam) + 泳 (yǒng, to swim).
Both characters relate to swimming; 游 is broader (also used in 旅游, to travel) while 泳 is specific to swimming strokes.
游泳馆 (yóuyǒngguǎn) is an indoor swimming pool; 游泳池 (yóuyǒngchí) is a swimming pool.
The phrase 孩子们夏天学游泳 uses 夏天 (xiàtiān, summer) as a time adverb directly before the verb — no preposition needed.

cards.spanishPhrase: Hago yoga cada mañana para relajarme.
cards.chinesePhrase: 我每天早上做瑜伽放松。
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ měitiān zǎoshang zuò yújiā fàngsōng.
cards.grammarNote:
瑜伽 (yújiā) is a phonetic loanword from Sanskrit 'yoga' — the characters 瑜 (yú, fine jade) and 伽 (jiā, a Buddhist transliteration character) carry no semantic meaning here.
Chinese has many Sanskrit-origin words via Buddhism.
Yoga is typically done with 做 (zuò, to do): 做瑜伽.
The phrase 每天早上做瑜伽放松 strings two actions together — 做瑜伽 (do yoga) then 放松 (relax) — as a result or purpose: 'do yoga to relax.'

cards.spanishPhrase: El ejercicio diario es importante para todos.
cards.chinesePhrase: 每天健身对每个人都很重要。
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Měitiān jiànshēn duì měi gè rén dōu hěn zhòngyào.
cards.grammarNote:
健身 (jiànshēn) literally means 'to strengthen the body' — 健 (jiàn, healthy/strong) + 身 (shēn, body).
It specifically refers to gym workouts and fitness training.
Compare with broader exercise terms: 运动 (yùndòng, sports/exercise broadly), 锻炼 (duànliàn, to train/exercise for fitness).
健身房 (jiànshēnfáng) is a gym: 我去健身房健身.
The phrase 每天健身对每个人都很重要 uses 对…重要 (important for/to…), a very common structure.

cards.spanishPhrase: Me encanta viajar a nuevos lugares.
cards.chinesePhrase: 我很喜欢去新地方旅行。
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ hěn xǐhuan qù xīn dìfang lǚxíng.
cards.grammarNote:
旅行 (lǚxíng) is 旅 (lǚ, to travel/journey) + 行 (xíng, to go/travel).
The character 旅 depicts a person following a flag, evoking group travel or military march.
Compare 旅行 (travel, the activity) with 旅游 (lǚyóu, tourism/sightseeing) — 旅游 implies leisure touring while 旅行 is more general.
旅行社 (lǚxíngshè) is a travel agency.
The phrase 去新地方旅行 (travel to new places) uses 去…旅行 as a common pattern.

cards.spanishPhrase: Fuimos a acampar en el bosque.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒmen qù sēnlín lǐ lùyíng le.
cards.grammarNote:
露营 (lùyíng) is 露 (lù, to be exposed outdoors/dew) + 营 (yíng, camp).
营 appears in 军营 (jūnyíng, military camp) and 营地 (yíngdì, campsite).
The character 露 (露天, open-air) reinforces the outdoor, uncovered nature of camping.
The phrase 去森林里露营 uses 去 + location + 里 + verb: 'go into the forest to camp.' 里 (lǐ, inside) is used with enclosed or bounded spaces like forests, rooms, cities.

cards.spanishPhrase: Mi hermano juega videojuegos cada noche.
cards.chinesePhrase: 我弟弟每晚都玩电子游戏。
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ dìdi měi wǎn dōu wán diànzǐ yóuxì.
cards.grammarNote:
玩电子游戏 (wán diànzǐ yóuxì) breaks down as 玩 (wán, to play) + 电子 (diànzǐ, electronic) + 游戏 (yóuxì, game).
玩 is the key verb for playing games, toys, or leisure activities (not sports).
电子 (electronic) appears in 电子书 (e-book), 电子邮件 (email).
Colloquially, 打游戏 (dǎ yóuxì) or 玩游戏 are used for gaming.
游戏 alone can mean any game: 桌游 (board game), 小游戏 (mini game).

cards.spanishPhrase: Me relajo escuchando música tranquila.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ tōngguò tīng yīnyuè lái fàngsōng.
cards.grammarNote:
听音乐 (tīng yīnyuè) is 听 (tīng, to listen) + 音乐 (yīnyuè, music).
听 is used for all listening: 听课 (attend class), 听广播 (listen to radio), 听说 (hear that…/it is said).
The phrase 通过听音乐来放松 uses the 通过…来… (tōngguò…lái…) structure meaning 'by means of… to…': 通过练习来提高 (improve through practice).
来 here is not directional but indicates purpose — a common pattern in formal and written Chinese.

cards.spanishPhrase: Ella hace artesanías con sus hijos.
cards.chinesePhrase: 她和孩子们一起做手工艺。
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Tā hé háizimen yīqǐ zuò shǒugōngyì.
cards.grammarNote:
手工艺 (shǒugōngyì) is 手 (shǒu, hand) + 工 (gōng, work/craft) + 艺 (yì, art/skill) — 'handmade craft art.' A shorter form is 手工 (shǒugōng, handcraft): 做手工.
手工 also functions as an adjective: 手工制作 (shǒugōng zhìzuò, handmade).
The phrase 她和孩子们一起做手工艺 uses 和…一起 (hé…yīqǐ, together with…) — a common pattern: 我和朋友一起吃饭 (I ate with friends).

cards.spanishPhrase: Jugamos juegos de mesa los domingos.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒmen xīngqītiān wán zhuōyóu.
cards.grammarNote:
桌游 (zhuōyóu) is an abbreviation of 桌上游戏 (zhuō shàng yóuxì, games on the table) — 桌 (zhuō, table) + 游 (yóu, play/game).
This abbreviation style (taking the first character of each word) is common in modern Chinese: 手机 from 手提电话, 网购 from 网络购物.
The measure word for rounds of board games is 局 (jú): 玩了一局桌游.
The phrase 我们星期天玩桌游 — 星期天 (xīngqītiān) means Sunday (literally 'week's sky/day').

cards.spanishPhrase: Trabaja duro para ganar un buen salario.
cards.chinesePhrase: 他努力工作来赚更多钱。
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Tā nǔlì gōngzuò lái zhuàn gèng duō qián.
cards.grammarNote:
赚钱 (zhuànqián) is 赚 (zhuàn, to earn/profit) + 钱 (qián, money) — another verb-object compound.
赚 implies earning through effort or trade.
Compare: 挣钱 (zhèngqián, also to earn money, slightly more effort-focused) and 赚到钱 (zhuàn dào qián, succeed in earning money).
The phrase 努力工作来赚更多钱 uses the purpose structure 来 + verb: 'work hard in order to earn more money.' 更多 (gèng duō, even more) is a useful intensifier.

cards.spanishPhrase: Mi abuelo quiere jubilarse pronto.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ yéye xiǎng zǎodiǎn tuìxiū.
cards.grammarNote:
退休 (tuìxiū) is 退 (tuì, to retreat/step back) + 休 (xiū, to rest) — 'to step back and rest.' 休 appears in 休息 (xiūxi, to rest), 暑假 (shǔjià, summer break), and 休假 (xiūjià, to take leave).
The phrase 我爷爷想早点退休 uses 早点 (zǎodiǎn, a bit earlier/sooner) — 点 softens the adjective.
Compare: 晚点 (wǎndiǎn, a bit later), 多点 (duōdiǎn, a bit more).
爷爷 (yéye) specifically means paternal grandfather.

cards.spanishPhrase: Ella tiene un trabajo en la ciudad.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Tā zài chénglǐ yǒu gōngzuò.
cards.grammarNote:
有工作 (yǒu gōngzuò) is the straightforward existential structure 有 (yǒu, to have) + 工作 (gōngzuò, work/job).
Note that 工作 serves as both a noun (job) and a verb (to work): 我在工作 (I'm working), 这是我的工作 (this is my job).
The negation is 没有工作 (méiyǒu gōngzuò, not have a job) — 没有 is used to negate 有, not 不有.
This is a fundamental rule: 没有 negates possession and existence; 不 negates other verbs.

cards.spanishPhrase: Él lleva un mes desempleado.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Tā shīyè yǐjīng yī gè yuè le.
cards.grammarNote:
失业 (shīyè) is 失 (shī, to lose) + 业 (yè, occupation/industry) — 'to lose one's occupation.' 失 is a productive character: 失去 (shīqù, to lose), 失望 (shīwàng, disappointed), 失眠 (shīmián, insomnia).
业 appears in 职业 (zhíyè, profession), 行业 (hángyè, industry sector), 毕业 (bìyè, to graduate).
The phrase 他失业已经一个月了 uses 已经…了 (yǐjīng…le, already…) to indicate a state that has persisted: 'he has already been unemployed for a month.'

cards.spanishPhrase: Me gusta estudiar por la mañana.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ xǐhuān zài zǎoshang xuéxí.
cards.grammarNote:
学习 (xuéxí) is 学 (xué, to learn/imitate) + 习 (xí, to practice/review).
Together they encompass both receiving knowledge and practicing it.
Compare with 学 alone (to learn a skill: 学游泳) or 读书 (dúshū, to study/read books).
The phrase 我喜欢在早上学习 uses 在 + time: 在早上 (in the morning).
喜欢 + verb (to like to do something) is one of the most essential patterns: 喜欢学习, 喜欢跑步, 喜欢吃饭.

cards.spanishPhrase: Quiero aprender a cocinar.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ xiǎng xué zuòfàn.
cards.grammarNote:
学 (xué) as a standalone verb means 'to learn' a skill or subject — often followed directly by the object: 学中文, 学做饭, 学开车.
Compare with 学习 (xuéxí, to study broadly) and 教 (jiāo, to teach) — a natural pair: 学 and 教.
The phrase 我想学做饭 shows 想 + verb (to want to do something) and 学 + verb (to learn to do something) stacked together — this nesting is natural in Chinese: 我想学开车 (I want to learn to drive).

cards.spanishPhrase: Ella practica piano todos los días.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Tā měitiān liànxí gāngqín.
cards.grammarNote:
练习 (liànxí) is 练 (liàn, to drill/train) + 习 (xí, to practice/review).
Both characters carry the sense of repetitive practice to build skill.
练 alone can mean 'to practice': 练钢琴, 练习中文.
Compare 练习 (practice as a focused activity) with 练 (informal: just practice it).
The phrase 她每天练习钢琴 uses 每天 (měitiān, every day) before the verb — a frequency expression placed before the verb phrase, a key word-order rule.

cards.spanishPhrase: Él enseña chino en la escuela.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Tā zài xuéxiào jiāo Zhōngwén.
cards.grammarNote:
教 (jiāo) is a simple one-character verb meaning 'to teach.' Its pronunciation is jiāo (first tone) as a verb, but 教 can also be pronounced jiào in some compound words (e.g., 教育 jiàoyù, education; 教室 jiàoshì, classroom).
The basic pattern is 教 + person + subject/skill: 他教我中文 (he teaches me Chinese).
The example 他在学校教中文 uses 在 + location before the verb to indicate where the action happens: 在学校教, 在家学习, 在图书馆看书.

cards.spanishPhrase: Queremos construir una casa nueva.
cards.chinesePhrase: 我们想建造一栋新房子。
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒmen xiǎng jiànzào yī dòng xīn fángzi.
cards.grammarNote:
建造 (jiànzào) is a formal compound: 建 (jiàn, to establish/build) + 造 (zào, to make/create).
Both characters mean construction; together they emphasize large-scale building projects.
In everyday speech, 盖 (gài) or 建 alone is used: 盖房子 (build a house).
造 appears in 制造 (zhìzào, to manufacture), 创造 (chuàngzào, to create).
The phrase 我们想建造一栋新房子 — 栋 (dòng) is the measure word specifically for buildings and houses.

cards.spanishPhrase: Necesito arreglar mi bicicleta hoy.
cards.chinesePhrase: 我今天需要修理我的自行车。
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ jīntiān xūyào xiūlǐ wǒ de zìxíngchē.
cards.grammarNote:
修理 (xiūlǐ) is 修 (xiū, to repair/cultivate) + 理 (lǐ, to put in order).
Together they mean 'to repair and put in order.' 修 alone is very common: 修车 (fix a car), 修电脑 (fix a computer).
理 also appears in 整理 (zhěnglǐ, to tidy up) and 管理 (guǎnlǐ, to manage).
The phrase 我今天需要修理我的自行车 uses 需要 (xūyào, need to) + verb — compare with 要 (yào, want/will), 得 (děi, must), 应该 (yīnggāi, should).

cards.spanishPhrase: ¿Puedes hacer el desayuno hoy?
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Nǐ jīntiān néng zuò zǎofàn ma?
cards.grammarNote:
做 (zuò) is one of the most versatile verbs in Chinese, meaning 'to do, to make, to cook, to be (a profession).' Common collocations: 做饭 (cook), 做作业 (do homework), 做生意 (do business), 做梦 (dream).
Compare 做 (make/do) with 制作 (zhìzuò, to produce/manufacture, more formal) and 造 (zào, to create/build).
The question 你今天能做早饭吗 uses 能 (néng, can/able to) to ask about capability or possibility.

cards.spanishPhrase: Ella diseña ropa muy bonita.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Tā shèjì de yīfu hěn piàoliang.
cards.grammarNote:
设计 (shèjì) is 设 (shè, to set up/plan) + 计 (jì, to plan/calculate) — 'planned design.' Both characters relate to planning and intention.
计 appears in 计划 (jìhuà, plan), 计算 (jìsuàn, to calculate), 统计 (tǒngjì, statistics).
The phrase 她设计的衣服很漂亮 uses the attributive construction: [verb + 的 + noun] = 'clothes that she designed.' This 的 clause structure is essential: 他做的菜, 我买的书, 她教的课.

cards.spanishPhrase: Él vende frutas en el mercado.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Tā zài shìchǎng mài shuǐguǒ.
cards.grammarNote:
卖 (mài) means 'to sell' and is the one-character counterpart to 买 (mǎi, to buy).
A useful mnemonic: 卖 (mài, sell) has more strokes on top — it has something 'extra' (the goods being exchanged); 买 (mǎi, buy) is simpler.
Together, 买卖 (mǎimài) means 'business/trade.' The phrase 他在市场卖水果 uses the 在 + place + verb pattern for ongoing activity at a location: 在市场工作, 在图书馆学习.

cards.spanishPhrase: Quiero comprar pan esta mañana.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ jīntiān zǎoshang xiǎng mǎi miànbāo.
cards.grammarNote:
买 (mǎi) means 'to buy/purchase.' Distinguish by tone and stroke count from 卖 (mài, sell).
Common patterns: 买东西 (mǎi dōngxi, go shopping/buy things), 买单 (mǎidān, pay the bill), 网购 (wǎnggòu, online shopping).
The phrase 我今天早上想买面包 shows 想 + verb: 'I want to buy bread.' For completed purchase, add 了: 我买了面包 (I bought bread).
面包 (miànbāo, bread) literally means 'flour package.'

cards.spanishPhrase: ¿Puedo ayudarte con eso?
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ kěyǐ bāngzhù nǐ ma?
cards.grammarNote:
帮助 (bāngzhù) is 帮 (bāng, to help) + 助 (zhù, to assist) — a synonym compound.
In speech, 帮 alone is more common: 帮我 (help me), 帮一下 (give a hand).
帮助 is more formal or written.
The pattern 帮 + person + verb is natural: 帮我拿一下 (help me grab that).
The question 我可以帮助你吗 uses 可以 (kěyǐ, may/can) for polite permission — contrast with 能 (capability) and 会 (know how to).

cards.spanishPhrase: Ella dirige un equipo grande.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Tā guǎnlǐ yī gè dà tuánduì.
cards.grammarNote:
管理 (guǎnlǐ) is 管 (guǎn, to control/take charge of) + 理 (lǐ, to put in order).
管 alone can mean 'to mind/concern oneself with': 不管 (regardless), 管得着吗 (is it your business?).
管理 is used for managing organizations, teams, or systems.
The verb 带 (dài, to lead/bring) is another common management term: 带团队 (lead a team).
The phrase 她管理一个大团队 uses 大 (dà, big/large) as a simple modifier — no measure word needed with 一个.

cards.spanishPhrase: Nos reunimos cada lunes por la mañana.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒmen měi zhōuyī zǎoshang kāihuì.
cards.grammarNote:
开会 (kāihuì) is 开 (kāi, to open/start) + 会 (huì, meeting/gathering) — literally 'to open a meeting.' This verb-object compound is distinctly Chinese: the act of holding a meeting is expressed as 'opening' one.
Compare: 开门 (open a door), 开车 (drive a car), 开始 (start).
For attending a meeting, use 参加 (cānjiā): 参加会议.
The phrase 每周一早上开会 is a good model for recurring schedule expressions.

cards.spanishPhrase: Vamos a empezar la clase ahora.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒmen xiànzài kāishǐ shàngkè.
cards.grammarNote:
开始 (kāishǐ) is 开 (kāi, to open) + 始 (shǐ, to begin).
It functions as both a verb (to start) and a time noun (the beginning): 从开始到结束 (from beginning to end).
Compare 开始 (start an action) with 开 + object (open/start something specific: 开门, 开车).
Pattern: 开始 + verb phrase: 开始学中文, 开始工作.
The phrase 我们现在开始上课 — 上课 (shàngkè) means 'attend class/start class,' another verb-object compound.

cards.spanishPhrase: Necesito terminar este trabajo hoy.
cards.chinesePhrase: 我今天需要完成这项工作。
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ jīntiān xūyào wánchéng zhè xiàng gōngzuò.
cards.grammarNote:
完成 (wánchéng) is 完 (wán, to finish/complete) + 成 (chéng, to accomplish/become).
完 alone used as a verb complement means completion: 吃完 (finish eating), 做完 (finish doing), 学完 (finish learning).
完成 as a full verb is more formal: 完成任务 (complete a task), 完成目标 (achieve a goal).
The phrase 我今天需要完成这项工作 uses 这项 (zhè xiàng) — 项 (xiàng) is the measure word for items, tasks, or projects.

cards.spanishPhrase: Me gusta relajarme en el parque.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ xǐhuān zài gōngyuán fàngsōng.
cards.grammarNote:
放松 (fàngsōng) is 放 (fàng, to release/let go) + 松 (sōng, loose/relaxed).
The combination literally means 'release to looseness.' 放 is highly productive: 放心 (fàngxīn, relax one's mind/don't worry), 放假 (fàngjià, to have a holiday), 放弃 (fàngqì, to give up).
In the example 我喜欢在公园放松, 在公园 (in the park) is a location phrase placed before the verb — a key Chinese word order principle: location before action.

cards.spanishPhrase: Paso mucho tiempo en mis aficiones.
cards.chinesePhrase: 我花很多时间在爱好上。
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ huā hěn duō shíjiān zài àihào shàng.
cards.grammarNote:
花时间 (huā shíjiān) is 花 (huā, to spend/expend) + 时间 (shíjiān, time).
花 normally means 'flower,' but as a verb it means 'to spend' (time or money): 花钱 (spend money), 花时间 (spend time), 花精力 (expend energy).
The phrase 我花很多时间在爱好上 uses 在…上 (on/regarding…): 花时间在学习上 (spend time on studying).
This 在…上 structure expresses what you direct effort toward: 专注在工作上 (focus on work).

cards.spanishPhrase: Tu idea es muy creativa.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Nǐ de xiǎngfǎ hěn yǒu chuàngyì.
cards.grammarNote:
有创意的 (yǒu chuàngyì de) is literally 'having creativity/creative ideas' — 有 (yǒu, to have) + 创意 (chuàngyì, creative idea) + 的 (de, attributive particle).
This 有…的 pattern is common for expressing qualities: 有意思的 (interesting), 有意义的 (meaningful), 有帮助的 (helpful).
创 (chuàng) appears in 创造 (chuàngzào, create), 创业 (chuàngyè, start a business).
In casual speech, 很有创意 is used as a predicate: 你很有创意 (you're very creative).

cards.spanishPhrase: Este libro es muy interesante.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Zhè běn shū hěn yǒuqù.
cards.grammarNote:
有趣的 (yǒuqù de) is 有 (yǒu, to have) + 趣 (qù, interest/fun) + 的 — 'having interest/fun.' 趣 alone is literary; the common spoken word is 有趣 (yǒuqù, interesting/fun).
Compare 有趣 (intellectually interesting/fun) with 有意思 (yǒu yìsi, interesting, nuanced/meaningful) — they overlap but 有意思 often implies deeper interest or humor.
The phrase 这本书很有趣 uses 很 (hěn) before the stative verb 有趣 — 很 is required with most adjectives in predicative position without a comparative context.

cards.spanishPhrase: Mi trabajo es muy estresante a veces.
cards.chinesePhrase: 我的工作有时很有压力。
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ de gōngzuò yǒushí hěn yǒu yālì.
cards.grammarNote:
有压力的 (yǒu yālì de) is 有 (to have) + 压力 (yālì, pressure/stress) + 的.
压力 is 压 (yā, to press down) + 力 (lì, force/power) — 'pressing force.' Compare: 有压力 (to have stress) vs 没有压力 (to have no stress).
A related useful word is 压力大 (yālì dà, high-pressure, literally 'pressure big'), extremely common in modern Chinese: 工作压力很大.
The example phrase 我的工作有时很有压力 uses 有时 (yǒushí, sometimes) before the adjective.

cards.spanishPhrase: La reunión fue muy aburrida hoy.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Jīntiān de huìyì hěn wúliáo.
cards.grammarNote:
无聊的 (wúliáo de) is 无 (wú, without/lacking) + 聊 (liáo, chat/pass time) + 的 — 'without anything to chat about/pass time with,' hence boring.
无 is a literary/formal negation, contrasting with 没 (méi) in speech.
Other 无 words: 无聊 (boring), 无法 (unable to), 无论 (regardless).
聊 alone means 'to chat': 聊天 (liáotiān, to chat — literally 'chat sky').
The phrase 今天的会议很无聊 — note that 今天的 (today's) uses 的 to link time with noun.

cards.spanishPhrase: Su salario es bastante bueno.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Tā de gōngzī xiāngdāng búcuò.
cards.grammarNote:
工资 (gōngzī) is 工 (gōng, work/labor) + 资 (zī, resources/funds) — 'labor funds.' Alternative terms: 薪水 (xīnshuǐ, salary — more formal/literary), 薪资 (xīnzī, wages).
资 appears in 资金 (zījīn, capital/funds), 投资 (tóuzī, to invest), 工资单 (gōngzī dān, payslip).
The phrase 他的工资相当不错 uses 相当 (xiāngdāng, quite/fairly) as an intensifier, similar to 比较 (bǐjiào) and 挺 (tǐng): 相当好, 比较好, 挺好 all mean 'quite good.'

cards.spanishPhrase: ¿Puedes revisar mi horario de mañana?
cards.chinesePhrase: 你能看一下我明天的日程吗？
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Nǐ néng kàn yīxià wǒ míngtiān de rìchéng ma?
cards.grammarNote:
日程 (rìchéng) is 日 (rì, day/sun) + 程 (chéng, journey/process/schedule) — 'daily schedule/itinerary.' 程 appears in 行程 (xíngchéng, itinerary/travel route), 工程 (gōngchéng, engineering project), and 课程 (kèchéng, curriculum).
A related word is 时间表 (shíjiānbiǎo, timetable) or 安排 (ānpái, arrangements/schedule).
The phrase 你能看一下我明天的日程吗 uses 看一下 (kàn yīxià, take a look) — 一下 softens a request, making it brief and casual.

cards.spanishPhrase: Voy a tomar vacaciones la próxima semana.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ xià zhōu yào qù dù jiàqī.
cards.grammarNote:
假期 (jiàqī) is 假 (jià, leave/holiday) + 期 (qī, period/term).
假 has two readings: jià (vacation/leave: 假期, 放假) and jiǎ (false/fake: 假话, 假的).
期 appears in 学期 (xuéqī, semester), 期末 (qīmò, end of term).
Types of leave: 年假 (niánjià, annual leave), 病假 (bìngjià, sick leave), 产假 (chǎnjià, maternity leave).
The phrase 我下周要去度假期 — 度假 (dùjià, spend a holiday) is the common verb: 去度假 (go on vacation).

cards.spanishPhrase: Necesitamos un descanso ahora.
cards.chinesePhrase: 我们现在需要休息一下。
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒmen xiànzài xūyào xiūxi yīxià.
cards.grammarNote:
休息 (xiūxi) is 休 (xiū, to rest) + 息 (xī, breath/rest).
Together they form a synonym compound meaning 'to rest.' 休 alone appears in 午休 (wǔxiū, midday rest), 休假 (xiūjià, take leave).
息 appears in 消息 (xiāoxi, news), 利息 (lìxī, interest on a loan).
The phrase 我们现在需要休息一下 uses 一下 (yīxià, a little/for a moment) to soften and make the request more colloquial: 等一下 (wait a moment), 看一下 (take a look).

cards.spanishPhrase: Ella trabaja en una oficina grande.
cards.chinesePhrase: 她在一个大办公室工作。
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Tā zài yī gè dà bàngōngshì gōngzuò.
cards.grammarNote:
办公室 (bàngōngshì) is 办公 (bàngōng, to handle official business) + 室 (shì, room) — 'room for conducting business.' 办 (bàn, to handle/manage) appears in 办事 (bànshì, handle affairs), 办理 (bànlǐ, to process/handle), 举办 (jǔbàn, to hold an event).
室 (shì, room) appears in 教室 (jiàoshì, classroom), 浴室 (yùshì, bathroom), 卧室 (wòshì, bedroom).
The measure word for offices is 间 (jiān): 一间办公室.

cards.spanishPhrase: Me encanta trabajar al aire libre.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ xǐhuān zài hùwài gōngzuò.
cards.grammarNote:
户外 (hùwài) is 户 (hù, household/door) + 外 (wài, outside) — 'outside the household/door.' Compare 室外 (shìwài, outdoor, literally outside the room) and 野外 (yěwài, wilderness/open country).
外 appears in 外面 (wàimiàn, outside), 国外 (guówài, abroad), 外语 (wàiyǔ, foreign language).
The phrase 在户外工作 — 在 + location + verb is the standard pattern for ongoing activity at a place: 在公园跑步, 在家休息, 在户外运动.

cards.spanishPhrase: Tengo una entrevista mañana por la mañana.
cards.chinesePhrase: 我明天早上有一个面试。
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ míngtiān zǎoshang yǒu yī gè miànshì.
cards.grammarNote:
面试 (miànshì) is 面 (miàn, face/meet) + 试 (shì, to test/try) — 'to meet and test,' i.e., an interview.
面 appears in 面对 (miànduì, to face/confront), 见面 (jiànmiàn, to meet someone).
试 appears in 考试 (kǎoshì, exam), 尝试 (chángshì, to attempt).
The phrase 我明天早上有一个面试 — 有 + noun (to have a meeting/interview/appointment) is a common and natural pattern: 我有一个会议, 你有时间吗 (do you have time?).

cards.spanishPhrase: Mi compañero de trabajo es muy amable.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ de tóngshì fēicháng yǒuhǎo.
cards.grammarNote:
同事 (tóngshì) is 同 (tóng, same/together) + 事 (shì, matter/work/affair) — 'one who shares the same work.' 同 is very productive: 同学 (tóngxué, classmate — same study), 同屋 (tóngwū, roommate — same room), 同行 (tóngháng, person in the same trade).
The phrase 我的同事非常友好 — 友好 (yǒuhǎo, friendly) is 友 (yǒu, friend) + 好 (hǎo, good).
Distinguish 友好 (friendly attitude) from 友善 (yǒushàn, kind/amiable in character).

cards.spanishPhrase: El cliente quiere hablar con el jefe.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Gùkè xiǎng hé lǎobǎn shuōhuà.
cards.grammarNote:
顾客 (gùkè) is 顾 (gù, to look after/patronize) + 客 (kè, guest/visitor) — 'a guest who patronizes.' 客 alone means guest and appears in 客人 (kèrén, guest), 客气 (kèqi, polite/modest), 旅客 (lǚkè, traveler).
Another word for customer is 客户 (kèhù, client — often for business clients).
The measure word is 位 (wèi, respectful): 一位顾客.
The phrase 顾客想和老板说话 — 老板 (lǎobǎn, boss/owner) is a common informal term.

cards.spanishPhrase: Nuestro equipo trabaja muy bien junto.
cards.chinesePhrase: 我们的团队合作得很好。
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒmen de tuánduì hézuò de hěn hǎo.
cards.grammarNote:
团队 (tuánduì) is 团 (tuán, group/circle) + 队 (duì, team/line) — both characters convey the sense of a collective.
团 appears in 团结 (tuánjié, unity), 集团 (jítuán, group/corporation).
队 appears in 球队 (qiúduì, sports team), 队伍 (duìwu, troops/group).
A related word is 小组 (xiǎozǔ, small group).
The phrase 我们的团队合作得很好 — 合作 (hézuò, to cooperate) is 合 (to join) + 作 (to do/work).
合作得很好 uses the result complement pattern: verb + 得 + result.

cards.spanishPhrase: Estamos trabajando en un proyecto nuevo.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒmen zài zuò yī gè xīn xiàngmù.
cards.grammarNote:
项目 (xiàngmù) is 项 (xiàng, item/category) + 目 (mù, item/eye/heading) — both characters mean 'item' in a list or plan.
Together they refer to a project or program.
项 is also a measure word for tasks and items: 一项工作 (one task), 两项要求 (two requirements).
目 appears in 目标 (mùbiāo, goal/target), 目的 (mùdì, purpose).
A shorter alternative is 工程 (gōngchéng, project — with engineering implications) or 计划 (jìhuà, plan/project).

cards.spanishPhrase: La reunión empieza a las nueve.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Huìyì jiǔ diǎn kāishǐ.
cards.grammarNote:
会议 (huìyì) is 会 (huì, meeting/gathering) + 议 (yì, to discuss/deliberate) — a formal compound for a meeting or conference.
Compare 会议 (formal meeting, conference) with 开会 (kāihuì, to hold/attend a meeting, more action-focused) and 见面 (jiànmiàn, to meet a person, social).
议 appears in 议题 (yìtí, agenda item), 议员 (yìyuán, parliamentarian).
The phrase 会议九点开始 — no 在 needed before the time; time + 开始 is natural Chinese word order.

cards.spanishPhrase: ¿Qué haces en tu tiempo libre?
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Nǐ kòngxián shíjiān zuò shénme?
cards.grammarNote:
空闲时间 (kòngxián shíjiān) is 空闲 (kòngxián, free/unoccupied) + 时间 (shíjiān, time).
A shorter form is 空闲 alone or 业余时间 (yèyú shíjiān, leisure time — literally 'after-profession time').
空 (kōng/kòng) has two readings: kōng (empty: 空气, air) and kòng (free time/gap: 有空, to have free time).
The question 你空闲时间做什么 (what do you do in your free time) can be shortened to 你有空吗 (are you free?) — 有空 is the casual version.
Adverb form: 快乐地 (kuàilè de - happily). In Chinese, add 地 (de) after an adjective to form an adverb. E.g., '她快乐地笑着。' (She was smiling happily.)
cards.spanishPhrase: Hoy me siento muy feliz.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ jīntiān gǎndào hěn kuàilè.

cards.grammarNote: Adverb form: 悲伤地 (bēishāng de - sadly). Add 地 after the adjective. E.g., '他悲伤地点头。' (He nodded sadly.)
cards.spanishPhrase: Ella está triste porque extraña a su familia.
cards.chinesePhrase: 她因为想家而感到悲伤。
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Tā yīnwèi xiǎng jiā ér gǎndào bēishāng.

cards.spanishPhrase: Sintió mucha ira cuando lo escuchó.
cards.chinesePhrase: 他听到那件事时感到很愤怒。
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Tā tīngdào nà jiàn shì shí gǎndào hěn fènnù.
cards.grammarNote:
愤怒 (fènnù) = anger (noun or stative verb).
愤 = indignation/resentment, 怒 = rage/fury.
Both characters carry the heart radical 心 at the bottom — a common pattern for emotion characters.
愤怒 is more intense and literary than 生气 (shēngqì), which is the everyday word for being angry.
Used as a verb: 他很愤怒 (He is very angry).
As a noun in this card's phrase: 他的愤怒 (his anger).
让 (ràng) + person + verb = 'make someone do something': 让大家害怕 = made everyone afraid.

cards.spanishPhrase: El miedo no te deja avanzar.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Kǒngjù ràng nǐ wúfǎ qiánjìn.
cards.grammarNote:
恐惧 (kǒngjù) = fear (noun).
恐 = dread/terror, 惧 = to fear.
Both characters contain the heart radical 心 — the left component 忄 (vertical heart radical, 竖心旁) in 惧.
恐惧 is more literary/formal than the everyday 害怕 (hàipà = to be afraid of).
无法 (wúfǎ) = unable to / have no way to: 无法隐藏 = unable to hide.
Compare 不能 (bù néng = can't).

cards.spanishPhrase: Tengo mucho estrés en el trabajo.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ gōngzuò yālì hěn dà.
cards.grammarNote:
压力 (yālì) = pressure / stress.
压 = to press/suppress, 力 = strength/force.
The phrase structure [topic] + [verb phrase] + 很大 is a common Chinese pattern: 工作压力很大 = 'work stress is very big' (i.e., a lot of work stress).
Note: Chinese often uses 大 (big) and 小 (small) for intensity: 压力大 (a lot of stress), 压力小 (little stress), rather than 'much/little'.
Related word: 有压力 (yǒu yālì) = to have/feel stress.

cards.spanishPhrase: Su estado de ánimo cambió de repente.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Tā de xīnqíng tūrán biàn le.
cards.grammarNote:
心情 (xīnqíng) = mood / state of mind.
心 = heart, 情 = emotion/feeling.
情 contains the heart radical 忄 (竖心旁) on the left — a very common component in emotion words: 情感 (emotion), 激情 (passion), 友情 (friendship).
Pattern: 心情 + 很 + adjective = 'mood is very [adj]': 心情很好 (in a good mood), 心情很差 (in a bad mood), 心情很复杂 (in a complicated mood).
Note: Chinese does NOT say ~~我是好心情~~ — use 我心情很好, treating 心情 as the topic.
Adverb form: 平静地 (píngjìng de - calmly). Add 地 after the adjective. E.g., '她平静地回答。' (She answered calmly.)
cards.spanishPhrase: Respira hondo para mantener la calma.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Shēn hūxī, bǎochí píngjìng.

cards.spanishPhrase: Encontré paz en el campo.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ zài xiāngxia zhǎodào le hépíng.
cards.grammarNote:
和平 (hépíng) = peace (especially world peace / absence of war).
和 = harmony/together, 平 = even/calm/flat.
For inner peace, use 内心平静 (nèixīn píngjìng) or 平和 (pínghé = calm and peaceful, of a person's temperament).
渴望 (kěwàng) = to long for / yearn for.
渴 = thirsty (showing the intensity of the desire), 望 = to gaze/expect.
Together: 'thirsting for.' 都 (dōu) = all/both, requires a plural subject: 我们都 = we all.

cards.spanishPhrase: Todos gritaron de sorpresa.
cards.chinesePhrase: 大家都惊讶地叫了起来。
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Dàjiā dōu jīngyà de jiào le qǐlái.
cards.grammarNote:
惊讶 (jīngyà) = surprised / astonished.
惊 = to startle/be startled, 讶 = to be amazed.
惊 contains the heart radical 忄 (竖心旁) on the left — the emotion of being startled lives in the heart.
Near-synonyms: 吃惊 (chī jīng) = to be shocked/startled (literally 'eat a fright' — very colloquial), 惊喜 (jīngxǐ) = pleasant surprise.
满心 (mǎn xīn) = 'full heart / with all one's heart': 满心惊讶 = heart full of surprise.
This is a vivid, slightly literary expression.

cards.spanishPhrase: Ella baila con mucha pasión.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Tā tiàowǔ hěn yǒu jīqíng.
cards.grammarNote:
激情 (jīqíng) = passion / intense enthusiasm.
激 = to excite/stir up (water radical 氵), 情 = emotion (heart radical 忄).
The 情 character (emotion) contains the heart radical 忄 (竖心旁) — found in many emotion words: 激情, 心情, 情感, 友情.
充满激情地 = passionately (literally 'filled with passion [manner]').
This uses 地 to form an adverb from a phrase: 充满激情地说话 = speak passionately.
Note: 地 (de) as adverb marker is pronounced the same as 的 but written differently and used only before verbs.

cards.spanishPhrase: Tengo un gran deseo de viajar.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ fēicháng kěwàng qù lǚxíng.
cards.grammarNote:
渴望 (kěwàng) = to long for / desire / yearn.
Works as both verb and noun.
渴 = thirsty (water radical 氵 on left, suggesting physical craving), 望 = to gaze toward / hope for.
Together: 'to thirst for something' — a vivid image of intense desire.
As a verb, 渴望 directly precedes the object: 渴望旅行 = desire to travel, 渴望自由 = yearn for freedom.
Near-synonym: 希望 (xīwàng) = to hope (less intense, more general).

cards.spanishPhrase: El odio no trae nada bueno.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Chóuhèn bù huì dài lái hǎo shì.
cards.grammarNote:
仇恨 (chóuhèn) = hatred / deep hatred (noun).
仇 = enmity/grudge, 恨 = to hate/resent.
恨 contains the heart radical 忄 (竖心旁) on the left — hatred lives in the heart.
仇恨 is stronger and more formal than 恨 alone, often used for hatred between groups or for ideological/historical grievance.
只会 (zhǐ huì) = 'will only / can only': 只会伤害自己 = will only hurt yourself.
自己 (zìjǐ) = oneself / yourself — a reflexive pronoun that works for all persons: 伤害自己 (hurt oneself).

cards.spanishPhrase: El placer de comer es universal.
cards.chinesePhrase: 吃东西的快乐是共同的。
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Chī dōngxi de kuàilè shì gòngtóng de.
cards.grammarNote:
快乐 (kuàilè) = happiness / pleasure / joy (noun or adjective).
快 = fast/pleased (contains heart radical 忄 on the left), 乐 = music/joy.
Three near-synonyms for happy — distinguish carefully: • 快乐 (kuàilè) = general happiness, joy — used for deeper contentment or well-wishes (生日快乐!
Happy birthday!) • 高兴 (gāoxìng) = happy/pleased in the moment — 高 (high) + 兴 (spirit) → high spirits • 开心 (kāixīn) = cheerful, light-hearted happy — 开 (open) + 心 (heart) → open heart Note: 很高兴认识你 is a fixed polite expression for meeting people — 高兴 + verb is more natural in that context than 快乐.

cards.spanishPhrase: No quiero ver tu dolor.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ bù xiǎng kàndào nǐ de tòngkǔ.
cards.grammarNote:
痛苦 (tòngkǔ) = pain / suffering / anguish (can be physical or emotional).
痛 = pain/ache, 苦 = bitter/suffering.
Both characters are evocative: 痛 conveys acute pain, 苦 conveys prolonged bitterness or hardship.
Compare: 疼 (téng) = physical pain/ache (my knee hurts = 我膝盖疼), 难受 (nánshòu) = feel bad/uncomfortable (milder).
痛苦 is often used for deeper emotional or physical suffering: 内心痛苦 (inner anguish), 痛苦的经历 (painful experience).
非常 (fēicháng) = extremely / very much.
Stronger than 很 (very).

cards.spanishPhrase: Tengo mucha energía por la mañana.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ zǎoshang jīnglì hěn chōngpèi.
cards.grammarNote:
精力 (jīnglì) = energy / vitality (especially mental/physical vigor).
精 = refined/essence, 力 = strength/power.
Related: 精力充沛 (jīnglì chōngpèi) = full of energy (a common set phrase), 有精力 (yǒu jīnglì) = have energy.
没什么 (méi shénme) = 'not much / nothing much': 没什么精力 = not much energy.
什么 here softens the negation rather than asking 'what?' Compare 精力 (mental/physical vitality) vs 能量 (néngliàng = energy in a scientific/physical sense, or informal energy).

cards.spanishPhrase: Sentí un gran alivio al terminar.
cards.chinesePhrase: 完成之后我感到很宽慰。
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wánchéng zhīhòu wǒ gǎndào hěn kuānwèi.
cards.grammarNote:
放心 (fàngxīn) = to feel relieved / feel at ease (literally 'release the heart').
The everyday word for relief.
More formal alternatives: 宽慰 (kuānwèi, comfort/consolation) or 如释重负 (rú shì zhòng fù, 'as if a heavy burden has been lifted').
放 = release/let go, 心 = heart — putting your heart at ease.
考完试 = after finishing the exam.
完 as a resultative complement: 考完 (finished taking [the exam]).
Common pattern: V + 完 = finish doing [verb].

cards.spanishPhrase: Ella ama a su familia con todo el corazón.
cards.chinesePhrase: 她全心全意地爱她的家人。
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Tā quánxīn quányì de ài tā de jiārén.
cards.grammarNote:
爱 (ài) functions as both a noun ('love') and a verb ('to love').
As a verb it takes an object directly: 我爱你 (I love you), 我爱我的家人 (I love my family).
爱 vs 喜欢 (xǐhuān): 爱 is stronger and more emotionally deep; 喜欢 means 'like/enjoy.' You can 喜欢 food or hobbies, but 爱 implies deep affection.
The character 爱 contains the element 心 (heart) — literally carrying the heart inside.

cards.spanishPhrase: No odio a nadie en el mundo.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ bù hèn shìjiè shàng rènhé rén.
cards.grammarNote:
恨 (hèn) = to hate / resent (verb).
Contains the heart radical 忄 (竖心旁) on the left — hatred is felt in the heart.
Compare: 恨 (personal, emotional hatred) vs 仇恨 (chóuhèn) = deep hatred/enmity (more intense, often between groups).
被 (bèi) marks the passive voice: 被人欺骗 = 'be deceived by people'.
Structure: Subject + 被 + (agent) + verb.
The 被 construction often carries a negative or unwanted tone — something unpleasant happened to the subject.
欺骗 (qīpiàn) = to deceive/cheat.

cards.spanishPhrase: Me gusta mucho el café por la mañana.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ zǎoshang hěn xǐhuān hē kāfēi.
cards.grammarNote:
喜欢 (xǐhuān) = to like / enjoy.
One of the most common verbs in Chinese.
喜 = joy/delight (contains 口 mouth + 壴 a drum-like element, suggesting joyful noise), 欢 = happy/joyful.
Difference from near-synonyms: • 喜欢 (xǐhuān) = to like (general preferences, people, things, activities) • 爱 (ài) = to love (stronger, deeper affection) • 爱好 (àihào) = hobby / something you love to do (noun) 喜欢 + noun: 我喜欢这个地方 (I like this place) 喜欢 + verb phrase: 我喜欢吃辣的 (I like eating spicy food) Does NOT need a 是: never say ~~我是喜欢~~.

cards.spanishPhrase: Disfruto caminar por el parque.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ xiǎngshòu zài gōngyuán sànbù.
cards.grammarNote:
享受 (xiǎngshòu) = to enjoy / to savor.
享 = to receive/enjoy (as a privilege), 受 = to receive/accept.
More deliberate and savoring than 喜欢 — implies actively enjoying and appreciating something.
与 (yǔ) = with (more formal/written than 跟 gēn or 和 hé).
共度 (gòngdù) = to spend (time) together — 共 = together, 度 = to pass/spend (time).
时光 (shíguāng) = time / the flow of time (more poetic than 时间 shíjiān which is clock time).

cards.spanishPhrase: Espero que todo salga bien.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ xīwàng yīqiè shùnlì.
cards.grammarNote:
希望 (xīwàng) works as both a noun ('hope') and a verb ('to hope').
As a verb it is followed by a clause: 我希望你早日好起来 = I hope you get better soon.
希 = to hope/wish, 望 = to gaze/expect.
Together they express hopeful anticipation.
早日 (zǎorì) = soon / at an early date (literally 'early day').
A polite, caring expression.
好起来 = 'get better / recover' — 起来 (qǐlái) as a directional complement often signals improvement or an upward change of state.

cards.spanishPhrase: Me preocupa que llegues tarde.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ dānxīn nǐ huì chídào.
cards.grammarNote:
担心 (dānxīn) = to worry / be worried.
担 = to carry on one's shoulders / bear a burden, 心 = heart.
Literal meaning: to carry worry in the heart → to worry.
心 appears directly in this word — one of the most transparent heart-character combinations.
别 (bié) = don't (imperative, softer than 不要): 别担心 = Don't worry.
一切都好 = everything is fine.
一切 (yīqiè) = everything/all, always paired with 都 (all): 一切都好, 一切都会好的.

cards.spanishPhrase: Él intentó consolar a su amiga triste.
cards.chinesePhrase: 他尝试安慰他悲伤的朋友。
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Tā chángshì ānwèi tā bēishāng de péngyou.
cards.grammarNote:
安慰 (ānwèi) = comfort / to comfort / consolation.
Works as both verb and noun.
安 = peaceful/safe, 慰 = to console (contains 心 heart at bottom).
As a verb: 安慰她 = to comfort her.
As a noun: 需要安慰 = need comfort.
听到 (tīngdào) = 'to hear (and receive)' — 到 is a resultative complement showing the action reached its goal.
Compare 听 (trying to hear) vs 听到 (actually heard it).
消息 (xiāoxi) = news / information.
坏消息 = bad news, 好消息 = good news.

cards.spanishPhrase: El ruido fuerte me molesta mucho.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Hěn dà de zàoyīn hěn rěnǎo wǒ.
cards.grammarNote:
惹恼 (rěnǎo) = to annoy / irritate / provoke to anger.
惹 = to provoke/stir up, 恼 = annoyed/resentful.
恼 contains the heart radical 忄 (竖心旁) on the left — annoyance sits in the heart.
Note the 了 after 惹恼: marks that the annoyance has been completed/achieved — 'did (successfully) annoy.' Related: 烦 (fán) = to feel annoyed/troubled (milder), 烦人 (fán rén) = annoying (person or thing), 让人烦 = makes one annoyed.
噪音 (zàoyīn) = noise (unwanted sound) — 噪 = noisy, 音 = sound.

cards.spanishPhrase: Sus mentiras me irritan mucho.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Tā de huǎngyán ràng wǒ hěn jīnù.
cards.grammarNote:
激怒 (jīnù) = to infuriate / enrage.
Stronger than 惹恼 (annoy).
激 = to provoke/stir up, 怒 = rage/fury (contains 心 heart at bottom — rage erupts from the heart).
行为 (xíngwéi) = behavior / conduct.
他的行为 = his behavior.
了 after 激怒: resultative — the infuriation was achieved/completed.
Note the causative structure: [cause] + 激怒 + 了 + [person] = '[cause] infuriated [person]'.

cards.spanishPhrase: Sus palabras lo enojaron mucho.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Tā de huà shǐ tā fēicháng fènnù.
cards.grammarNote:
使愤怒 = to anger someone / make someone furious.
Structure: 使 + person + 愤怒.
愤怒 (fènnù) = anger/fury — 愤 = indignation (contains 心 heart below), 怒 = rage (also contains 心 heart below).
Both characters have the heart radical 心 at the bottom — intense anger comes from deep within.
不公平 (bù gōngpíng) = unfair/unjust.
公平 = fair, 不公平 = unfair (adding 不 negates).
待遇 (dàiyù) = treatment / conditions.
而 (ér) = 'and therefore' / 'as a result' — connects cause and effect in a formal way.

cards.spanishPhrase: El regalo la deleitó muchísimo.
cards.chinesePhrase: 那个礼物使她非常高兴。
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Nà gè lǐwù shǐ tā fēicháng gāoxìng.
cards.grammarNote:
高兴 (gāoxìng) = happy / glad / pleased.
高 = high/tall, 兴 = spirit/enthusiasm.
Literal: high spirits → happy.
One of the most common words for happy in Chinese.
Three near-synonyms for happy: • 高兴 (gāoxìng) — pleased/happy in the moment: 我很高兴认识你 (Pleased to meet you) • 开心 (kāixīn) — cheerful, open-hearted: 开 (open) + 心 (heart) • 快乐 (kuàilè) — deep joy/happiness: used for birthdays, general contentment 使 (shǐ) + person + 高兴 = 'make someone happy'.
Note: 这份礼物 = 'this gift' — 份 is the measure word for gifts, portions.

cards.spanishPhrase: Ella sonrió cuando lo vio llegar.
cards.chinesePhrase: 她看到他来了就微笑了。
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Tā kàndào tā lái le jiù wēixiào le.
cards.grammarNote:
微笑 (wēixiào) = smile (noun or verb).
微 = tiny/slight, 笑 = to laugh/smile.
Literal: a slight laugh → a smile.
Compare 笑 alone = to laugh or smile (broader), 大笑 (dà xiào) = to laugh loudly.
对 (duì) before a person = 'toward / to': 对我微笑 = 'smiled at me.' 对 marks the direction or target of an action.
Near-synonym: 笑了笑 (xiàole xiào) = smiled briefly — verb reduplication for a quick, light action.

cards.spanishPhrase: Nos hizo reír a todos.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Tā ràng wǒmen dàjiā dōu xiào le.
cards.grammarNote:
笑 (xiào) = to laugh / to smile (general).
The character depicts a person bending with joy — a person leaning forward laughing.
Variants: 大笑 (dàxiào) = laugh loudly, 苦笑 (kǔxiào) = bitter smile, 微笑 (wēixiào) = gentle smile, 嘲笑 (cháoxiào) = to mock/ridicule (laugh at).
一起 (yīqǐ) = together.
Placed before the verb: 一起笑 = laughed together.
了 after the verb: marks the completed event — they laughed (it happened).

cards.spanishPhrase: Ella suspiró profundamente antes de hablar.
cards.chinesePhrase: 她说话之前深深地叹了口气。
