cards.spanishPhrase: Estoy preparando la cena para todos.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ zài wèi dàjiā zhǔnbèi wǎnfàn.

cards.spanishPhrase: Planeo viajar a China el año que viene.
cards.chinesePhrase: 我计划明年去中国旅行。
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ jìhuà míngnián qù Zhōngguó lǚxíng.
cards.grammarNote:
计划 (jìhuà) functions as both a verb (to plan) and a noun (a plan): 有计划 (yǒu jìhuà, to have a plan), 按计划 (àn jìhuà, according to plan).
As a verb, it is followed by an infinitive-like verb phrase: 计划明年去中国旅行 (plan to travel to China next year).
Note that Chinese has no infinitive form—verbs follow directly.
A synonym is 打算 (dǎsuàn, to intend/plan), which is more colloquial and typically only verbal, while 计划 serves as both verb and noun in formal contexts.

cards.spanishPhrase: Imagina vivir en otra ciudad.
cards.chinesePhrase: 你能想象住在另一个城市吗？
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Nǐ néng xiǎngxiàng zhù zài lìng yī gè chéngshì ma?
cards.grammarNote:
想象 (xiǎngxiàng) pairs 想 (xiǎng, to think/want) + 象 (xiàng, image/elephant/to resemble), literally 'to think in images'—a vivid etymology for imagination.
It is transitive and can take a noun object or a clause: 想象一下 (xiǎngxiàng yīxià, just imagine), 想象住在另一个城市 (imagine living in another city).
The example 你能想象住在另一个城市吗？uses the polar question pattern verb + 吗 (ma) for yes/no questions.
想 alone means 'to think/miss/want,' showing how compounding with 象 narrows to imagining.
比较 can be a verb (to compare: 比较两个方法) or an adverb meaning 'relatively / fairly': 这比较难 (This is fairly difficult). For direct A vs. B comparisons, use the 比 structure.
cards.spanishPhrase: Compara los dos precios antes de comprar.
cards.chinesePhrase: 买之前先比较一下两个价格。
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Mǎi zhīqián xiān bǐjiào yīxià liǎng gè jiàgé.

cards.grammarNote: 走 broadly means 'to walk / to go / to leave.' As a directional complement: 走过来 (walk over here), 走进去 (walk inside).
cards.spanishPhrase: Camino al parque cada mañana.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ měitiān zǎoshàng zǒu dào gōngyuán.

cards.grammarNote: 跑 pairs with directional complements: 跑过来 (run over here), 跑出去 (run out). 跑步 is the noun/verb compound for jogging/running as exercise.
cards.spanishPhrase: Corro treinta minutos cada mañana.
cards.chinesePhrase: 我每天早上跑三十分钟。
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ měitiān zǎoshàng pǎo sānshí fēnzhōng.

cards.grammarNote: 跳 also appears in 跳舞 (to dance) and 跳绳 (jump rope).
cards.spanishPhrase: El niño salta de alegría.
cards.chinesePhrase: 那个孩子高兴地跳起来。
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Nàge háizi gāoxìng de tiào qǐlái.

cards.spanishPhrase: El avión vuela hacia Pekín.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Fēijī fēi xiàng Běijīng.
cards.grammarNote:
飞 (fēi) is a single-character verb meaning to fly; its pictographic origin depicts a bird in flight with spread wings.
It is used for aircraft, birds, and figuratively for fast movement: 飞快 (fēikuài, extremely fast), 飞奔 (fēibēn, to run at full speed).
The compound 飞机 (fēijī, airplane) literally means 'flying machine': 飞 (fly) + 机 (jī, machine).
In the example 飞机飞向北京, the directional verb complement 飞向 (fēi xiàng, fly toward) uses 向 (xiàng, toward/in the direction of) to specify direction.
骑 is used for riding astride: 骑自行车 (ride a bicycle), 骑马 (ride a horse). For vehicles you sit inside, use 坐 (坐车, 坐飞机).
cards.spanishPhrase: Monto en bicicleta al trabajo cada día.
cards.chinesePhrase: 我每天骑自行车去上班。
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ měitiān qí zìxíngchē qù shàngbān.

cards.grammarNote: 回来 is a directional complement (movement toward the speaker). 回去 (return away from speaker). 回 alone means 'to return to a place.'
cards.spanishPhrase: ¿A qué hora vuelves a casa hoy?
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Nǐ jīntiān jǐ diǎn huí lái?

cards.grammarNote: 出去 is a directional complement: 出 (exit) + 去 (away from speaker). The complement can be split by a verb: 走出去 (walk out).
cards.spanishPhrase: Voy a salir un momento, espérame.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ chū qù yīxià, děng deng wǒ.

cards.grammarNote: 进来 is a directional complement: 进 (enter) + 来 (toward speaker). 进去 means to go in (away from speaker). Can be split: 走进来 (walk in here).
cards.spanishPhrase: Por favor, entra y siéntate.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Qǐng jìn lái zuò ba.

cards.grammarNote: 上来 is a compound directional complement: 上 (up) + 来 (toward speaker). If movement is away: 上去. Used literally (climb up) or figuratively.
cards.spanishPhrase: Sube aquí, hay una buena vista.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Shàng lái ba, zhèlǐ jǐngsè hěn hǎo.

cards.grammarNote: 下去 is a compound directional complement: 下 (down) + 去 (away from speaker). Also used figuratively: 继续下去 (continue on).
cards.spanishPhrase: Baja con cuidado, las escaleras son resbaladizas.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Xiǎoxīn xià qù, lóutī hěn huá.

cards.grammarNote: 经过 takes a place as its object: 经过公园 (pass by the park). Also means 'after / through (a process)': 经过努力 (after hard work).
cards.spanishPhrase: Paso por la tienda camino a casa.
cards.chinesePhrase: 我回家的路上经过那家店。
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ huí jiā de lùshàng jīngguò nà jiā diàn.

cards.grammarNote: 睡觉 is a verb-object compound. 睡 is the verb; 觉 is the object. To insert a duration or modifier, place it between: 睡了八个小时的觉 or more colloquially 睡了八个小时.
cards.spanishPhrase: Duermo ocho horas cada noche.
cards.chinesePhrase: 我每天晚上睡八个小时觉。
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ měitiān wǎnshàng shuì bā gè xiǎoshí jiào.

cards.grammarNote: 起床 is a verb-object compound: 起 (rise) + 床 (bed). Time expressions precede: 我七点起床 (I get up at 7).
cards.spanishPhrase: Me levanto a las siete cada día.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ měitiān qī diǎn qǐ chuáng.

cards.grammarNote: 洗澡 is a verb-object compound: 洗 (wash) + 澡 (bath). Duration is inserted: 洗了二十分钟的澡 (showered for 20 minutes).
cards.spanishPhrase: Me ducho todas las mañanas.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ měitiān zǎoshàng xǐ zǎo.

cards.grammarNote: 刷牙 is a verb-object compound: 刷 (brush) + 牙 (teeth).
cards.spanishPhrase: Me cepillo los dientes después de comer.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ chīfàn hòu shuā yá.

cards.grammarNote: 玩 can stand alone or take an object: 玩游戏 (play games), 玩得很开心 (had a great time). 玩儿 is the Beijing-dialect variant.
cards.spanishPhrase: Los niños juegan juntos en el parque.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Háizimen zài gōngyuán yīqǐ wán.

cards.grammarNote: 工作 functions as both a verb (to work) and a noun (work / job). As a noun: 我的工作很忙 (my work is busy).
cards.spanishPhrase: Trabajo en una empresa grande.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ zài yī jiā dà gōngsī gōngzuò.

cards.grammarNote: 上班 is a verb-object compound: 上 (go to/attend) + 班 (work shift). Opposite: 下班 (get off work). 上班族 means 'office workers.'
cards.spanishPhrase: Voy al trabajo en metro cada día.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ měitiān zuò dìtiě shàng bān.

cards.grammarNote: 下班 is a verb-object compound: 下 (leave/descend from) + 班 (work shift). Opposite: 上班 (go to work).
cards.spanishPhrase: Salgo del trabajo a las seis.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ liù diǎn xià bān.

cards.grammarNote: 上学 is a verb-object compound meaning to attend school (as a student). Opposite: 放学 (school lets out). 上课 means 'attend class.'
cards.spanishPhrase: Mi hijo va a la escuela a las ocho.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ de háizi bā diǎn qù shàng xué.

cards.grammarNote: 看书 is a verb-object compound: 看 (look at/read) + 书 (book). For reading specific material, 看 can take different objects: 看报纸 (read newspaper), 看小说 (read a novel).
cards.spanishPhrase: Leo un libro antes de dormir.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ shuìjiào qián kàn shū.

cards.grammarNote: 看电视 is a verb-object compound. Duration goes between verb and object or after: 看了两个小时电视 (watched TV for two hours).
cards.spanishPhrase: Veo la televisión después de cenar.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ chī wán wǎnfàn hòu kàn diànshì.

cards.english: to go online / to surf the internet
cards.grammarNote: 上网 is a verb-object compound: 上 (go onto) + 网 (net/internet).
cards.spanishPhrase: Navego por internet en mi teléfono.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ yòng shǒujī shàng wǎng.

cards.grammarNote: 打游戏 is a verb-object compound. 打 here is a general-purpose action verb used with many activities: 打球 (play ball), 打牌 (play cards).
cards.spanishPhrase: Me gusta jugar videojuegos por la noche.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ xǐhuān wǎnshang dǎ yóuxì.

cards.grammarNote: 开 means to open (a door, a box) and to turn on (a light, a device). Resultative form: 开开 or more commonly 打开 (open up, turn on).
cards.spanishPhrase: Por favor, abre la ventana.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Qǐng nǐ bǎ chuānghù kāi kāi.

cards.grammarNote: 关 means to close and to turn off (lights, devices). Resultative form: 关上 (successfully shut). Opposite of 开.
cards.spanishPhrase: Cierra la puerta, por favor.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Qǐng bǎ mén guān shàng.

cards.grammarNote: 用 can be a main verb (use something) or a prepositional coverb: 用筷子吃饭 (eat with chopsticks). As a main verb: 你用什么方法？(What method do you use?)
cards.spanishPhrase: Uso el teléfono todos los días.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ měitiān dōu yòng shǒujī.

cards.grammarNote: 拿 implies holding something in the hand. Often used in 把 constructions: 把书拿来 (bring the book). Compare 带 (bring along / carry with you).
cards.spanishPhrase: Toma el libro de la mesa.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Bǎ zhuōzi shàng de shū ná guòlái.

cards.grammarNote: 带 implies bringing/taking something along on a journey. 带 + person means to take/bring someone: 带朋友来 (bring a friend). Compare 拿 (hold in hand).
cards.spanishPhrase: Trae tu paraguas hoy.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Jīntiān jìde dài sǎn.

cards.english: to send / to deliver (as a gift)
cards.grammarNote: 送 means both to send/deliver and to give as a gift. Pattern: 送给 + person + object: 我送给他一本书 (I gave him a book as a gift). Also: to see someone off (送他去机场).
cards.spanishPhrase: Le regalé flores a mi mamá.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ sòng gěi māma yī shù huā.

cards.grammarNote: 推 often appears in 把 constructions: 把门推开 (push the door open). Resultative complement 推开 means 'push [it] open.'
cards.spanishPhrase: Empuja la puerta para abrirla.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Yòng lì tuī kāi zhè shàn mén.

cards.grammarNote: 拉 means to pull. Common collocations: 拉门 (pull a door), 拉手 (hold hands / pull by the hand). Also used for playing string instruments: 拉小提琴 (play violin).
cards.spanishPhrase: Jala la cuerda con fuerza.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Yòng lì bǎ shéngzi lā guòlái.

cards.grammarNote: 扔 means to throw or toss. 扔掉 means to throw away/discard. 把垃圾扔掉 (throw away the trash) is a common 把 construction.
cards.spanishPhrase: Lanza la pelota hacia acá.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Bǎ qiú rēng guòlái gěi wǒ.

cards.grammarNote: 接 means to catch (a thrown object), to receive (a call: 接电话), or to pick someone up (接人). It is the complement pair of 送 in 送…接… (see off … pick up …).
cards.spanishPhrase: Atrapa la pelota con las manos.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Yòng shǒu bǎ qiú jiē zhù.

cards.grammarNote: 打 is an extremely versatile verb: hit (打人), play sports (打球), make a phone call (打电话), play cards (打牌), inject (打针). Context determines the specific meaning.
cards.spanishPhrase: No golpees a tu hermano.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Bùyào dǎ nǐ dìdi.

cards.grammarNote: 踢 collocates with ball sports: 踢足球 (play soccer). 踢 + directional complement: 踢开 (kick open).
cards.spanishPhrase: Le pateó la pelota a su amigo.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Tā bǎ qiú tī gěi le péngyǒu.

cards.grammarNote: 画 takes the thing drawn as its object: 画画 (draw a picture—note verb and noun are the same character), 画地图 (draw a map).
cards.spanishPhrase: Me gusta dibujar flores bonitas.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ xǐhuān huà piàoliang de huā.

cards.grammarNote: 写 takes the written product as its object: 写字 (write characters), 写文章 (write an essay). Can be used with 把: 把名字写下来 (write down the name).
cards.spanishPhrase: Escribe tu nombre aquí, por favor.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Qǐng zài zhèlǐ xiě nǐ de míngzi.

cards.grammarNote: 出生 is intransitive. Time and place use 在...出生 or 出生于...: 他出生在北京 (He was born in Beijing).
cards.spanishPhrase: Nació en una ciudad grande.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Tā chūshēng zài yī gè dà chéngshì.

cards.grammarNote: 结婚 is a verb-object compound: 结 (form/tie) + 婚 (marriage). To marry someone: 和...结婚 or 跟...结婚: 她和他结婚了 (She married him). NOT 结婚他.
cards.spanishPhrase: Se casaron el año pasado.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Tāmen qùnián jié hūn le.

cards.grammarNote: 离婚 is a verb-object compound: 离 (leave/separate) + 婚 (marriage). To divorce someone: 和...离婚.
cards.spanishPhrase: Se divorciaron después de diez años.
cards.chinesePhrase: 他们结婚十年后离婚了。
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Tāmen jié hūn shí nián hòu lí hūn le.

cards.grammarNote: 毕业 is a verb-object compound: 毕 (finish/complete) + 业 (studies/career). To graduate from: 从...毕业 or 毕业于...: 他从北大毕业 (He graduated from Peking University).
cards.spanishPhrase: Me gradué de la universidad el año pasado.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ qùnián cóng dàxué bì yè le.

cards.grammarNote: 搬家 is a verb-object compound: 搬 (move) + 家 (home). To move to a place: 搬到...去: 我搬到上海去了 (I moved to Shanghai).
cards.spanishPhrase: Me mudé a una casa nueva.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ bān jiā dào xīn fángzi lǐ le.

cards.grammarNote: 购物 is formal/written; 买东西 (buy things) is more colloquial. 网上购物 = online shopping.
cards.spanishPhrase: Fui de compras con mi amiga.
cards.chinesePhrase: 我和朋友一起去购物了。
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ hé péngyǒu yīqǐ qù gòuwù le.

cards.grammarNote: 点菜 is a verb-object compound: 点 (select/order) + 菜 (dish/food). Commonly heard in restaurants. 点 can also be used alone: 你点什么？(What are you ordering?)
cards.spanishPhrase: ¿Ya pediste la comida?
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Nǐ diǎn cài le ma?

cards.english: to pay the bill / to check out
cards.grammarNote: 结账 is a verb-object compound used in restaurants and shops. Common phrase: 我来结账 (I'll get the check) or 结账！(Check, please!)
cards.spanishPhrase: Yo pago la cuenta hoy.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Jīntiān wǒ lái jié zhàng.

cards.grammarNote: 旅游 means to travel for leisure/tourism. 旅行 is more general and can include non-leisure travel. Both can stand alone or take 去 + destination: 去欧洲旅游.
cards.spanishPhrase: Quiero viajar a Japón el próximo año.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ xiǎng míngnián qù Rìběn lǚyóu.

cards.grammarNote: 参加 takes events as its object: 参加会议 (attend a meeting), 参加比赛 (take part in a competition). For attending a place (school, work), use 上 or 去.
cards.spanishPhrase: ¿Vas a participar en la competencia?
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Nǐ yào cānjiā bǐsài ma?

cards.grammarNote: 举行 is used for formal events: 举行婚礼 (hold a wedding), 举行比赛 (hold a competition). More colloquial: 办 (举办 is a close synonym).
cards.spanishPhrase: Mañana se celebra la reunión.
cards.chinesePhrase: 明天举行一个重要的会议。
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Míngtiān jǔxíng yī gè zhòngyào de huìyì.

cards.grammarNote: 分享 means to share (information, experiences, things). Pattern: 分享 + thing + 给 + person, or 跟...分享...: 我跟大家分享一个故事 (I'll share a story with everyone).
cards.spanishPhrase: Quiero compartir una historia contigo.
cards.chinesePhrase: 我想跟你分享一个故事。
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ xiǎng gēn nǐ fēnxiǎng yī gè gùshi.

cards.grammarNote: 邀请 takes a person as object, optionally followed by purpose: 邀请他来参加聚会 (invite him to the party). 请 alone is a shorter colloquial form.
cards.spanishPhrase: Te invito a mi fiesta de cumpleaños.
cards.chinesePhrase: 我邀请你来参加我的生日聚会。
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ yāoqǐng nǐ lái cānjiā wǒ de shēngrì jùhuì.

cards.grammarNote: 拒绝 can take a noun or a verb phrase as object: 拒绝邀请 (refuse an invitation), 拒绝帮忙 (refuse to help).
cards.spanishPhrase: Rechazó la invitación con amabilidad.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Tā lǐmào de jùjué le yāoqǐng.

cards.grammarNote: 改变 can be transitive (改变想法, change one's mind) or intransitive (情况改变了, the situation has changed). 变 alone also means 'to change/become': 天气变冷了 (it's gotten cold).
cards.spanishPhrase: El tiempo ha cambiado mucho.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Tiānqì gǎibiàn le hěn duō.

cards.grammarNote: 大 precedes the noun it modifies: 大房子 (big house). It also combines into compounds: 大学 (university), 大人 (adult). Reduplicated as 大大 it acts as an adverb meaning 'greatly' (大大提高 = greatly improve).
cards.spanishPhrase: Vivo en una casa muy grande.
cards.chinesePhrase: 我住在一个很大的房子里。
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ zhù zài yī gè hěn dà de fángzi lǐ.

cards.grammarNote: 少 (shǎo) describes a small quantity. It contrasts with 多 (many). As a verb it means 'to lack': 少一个人 (one person short). Don't confuse with 小 (xiǎo, small in size).
cards.spanishPhrase: Hay pocas personas en la tienda.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Diàn lǐ de rén hěn shǎo.

cards.grammarNote: 长 (cháng) = long (dimension/time). As a verb it is pronounced zhǎng (to grow): 长大 (to grow up). Context distinguishes the two readings.
cards.spanishPhrase: Este camino es muy largo.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Zhè tiáo lù hěn cháng.

cards.grammarNote: 短 refers to length or duration, not height. For a short person use 矮 (ǎi). 短 + 暂 = 短暂 (brief, transient).
cards.spanishPhrase: El video es muy corto, dura dos minutos.
cards.chinesePhrase: 这个视频很短，只有两分钟。
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Zhège shìpín hěn duǎn, zhǐyǒu liǎng fēnzhōng.

cards.grammarNote: 高 describes both physical height (高楼 tall building) and degree (高兴 happy, 高温 high temperature). For people, 高 means tall; for objects it can mean either tall or high.
cards.spanishPhrase: Mi hermano es muy alto.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ gēgē zhǎng de hěn gāo.

cards.grammarNote: 低 is the antonym of 高. It covers physical height and abstract level: 低温 (low temperature), 低价 (low price). For a short person, use 矮 rather than 低.
cards.spanishPhrase: El precio es muy bajo aquí.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Zhèlǐ de jiàgé hěn dī.

cards.grammarNote: 重 (zhòng) = heavy / serious. As a verb it is pronounced chóng (to repeat, to do again): 重做 (redo). The adjective zhòng also extends to abstract weight: 重要 (important), 严重 (serious/severe).
cards.spanishPhrase: Esta maleta está muy pesada.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Zhège xiāngzi hěn zhòng.

cards.grammarNote: 轻 = light in weight. It also means 'gentle' or 'minor': 轻伤 (minor injury), 轻声 (softly). Don't confuse with 亮 (liàng, bright/luminous) when talking about light as illumination.
cards.spanishPhrase: Esta mochila es muy ligera.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Zhège shūbāo hěn qīng.

cards.grammarNote: 远 functions both as an adjective (很远 very far) and as a complement: 走得很远 (walked very far). The structure X 离 Y 很远 (X is far from Y) uses 离 to express distance between two points.
cards.spanishPhrase: La escuela está lejos de mi casa.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Xuéxiào lí wǒ jiā hěn yuǎn.

cards.grammarNote: 近 is the antonym of 远. Use X 离 Y 很近 to say X is close to Y. 近 also appears in time: 最近 (recently/lately).
cards.spanishPhrase: El supermercado está muy cerca.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Chāoshì lí zhèlǐ hěn jìn.

cards.grammarNote: 坏 means bad, broken, or spoiled: 坏人 (bad person), 电脑坏了 (the computer broke). As a result complement it intensifies a verb: 累坏了 (exhausted), 笑坏了 (laughed to death).
cards.spanishPhrase: El tiempo estuvo muy malo hoy.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Jīntiān tiānqì hěn huài.

cards.grammarNote: 新 precedes a noun or verb: 新书 (new book), 新来的 (newly arrived). As a prefix it contrasts with 旧 (old/used). 新鲜 (xīnxiān) = fresh (food/ideas).
cards.spanishPhrase: Compré un teléfono nuevo ayer.
cards.chinesePhrase: 我昨天买了一部新手机。
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ zuótiān mǎi le yī bù xīn shǒujī.

cards.grammarNote: 旧 describes objects that are old or used, not people. For an elderly person use 老 (lǎo); for age in years use 岁 (suì). 旧 also means 'former': 旧址 (former address).
cards.spanishPhrase: Este sofá está viejo, hay que cambiarlo.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Zhè zhāng shāfā hěn jiù, gāi huàn le.

cards.grammarNote: 快 is both an adjective (很快 very fast) and an adverb meaning 'soon': 快来了 (coming soon). As a complement: 走得很快 (walk fast). 快点 = hurry up.
cards.spanishPhrase: Este tren es muy rápido.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Zhè liè huǒchē pǎo de hěn kuài.

cards.grammarNote: 慢 is the antonym of 快. Use 慢点 to ask someone to slow down. In the degree complement pattern: 说得太慢了 (speaks too slowly). 慢慢 = slowly, gradually.
cards.spanishPhrase: Habla más lento, por favor.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Qǐng nǐ shuō màn yīdiǎn.

cards.grammarNote: 难 (nán) = difficult. 难 + verb = hard to do: 难学 (hard to learn), 难说 (hard to say). Don't confuse with 男 (nán, male) — same pronunciation, different character. As a verb 难 can mean 'to make things difficult for someone'.
cards.spanishPhrase: Este examen fue muy difícil.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Zhè cì kǎoshì hěn nán.

cards.grammarNote: 容易 (róngyì) is a two-syllable adjective meaning easy. 容易 + verb = easy to do: 容易忘 (easy to forget). It can also express likelihood: 这样容易出错 (this way mistakes are likely).
cards.spanishPhrase: Esta tarea es muy fácil.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Zhè dào tí hěn róngyì.

cards.grammarNote: 漂亮 (piàoliang) describes physical attractiveness of people or things. 亮 is typically read in neutral (light) tone in spoken Chinese: piàoliang. 美丽 (měilì) is more literary.
cards.spanishPhrase: Ese vestido es muy bonito.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Nà jiàn qúnzi hěn piàoliang.

cards.spanishPhrase: Tu hija es muy inteligente.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Nǐ nǚ'ér hěn cōngmíng.
cards.grammarNote:
聪明 (cōngmíng) is a disyllabic adjective: 聪 (cōng, quick of hearing/sharp) + 明 (míng, bright/clear), together meaning mentally sharp and perceptive.
聪 contains the ear radical 耳 (ěr), reflecting the original sense of keen hearing as a marker of intelligence.
Chinese adjectives function directly as predicates with 很 (hěn): 你女儿很聪明—no verb 'to be' is needed.
As an attributive modifier before a noun, 的 (de) is added: 聪明的学生 (cōngmíng de xuésheng, a smart student).
Antonyms include 笨 (bèn, stupid/clumsy) and 愚蠢 (yúchǔn, foolish).
有趣 = interesting/amusing. The structure 对…感兴趣 (gǎn xìngqù) = to be interested in something: 我对历史感兴趣. 有意思 (yǒu yìsi) is a more colloquial synonym.
cards.spanishPhrase: Esta película es muy interesante.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Zhè bù diànyǐng hěn yǒuqù.

cards.grammarNote: 无聊 describes both a boring thing and the feeling of being bored (having nothing to do). 我很无聊 = I'm bored. For a person who is tedious or trivial, 无聊 also works colloquially.
cards.spanishPhrase: La clase de hoy fue muy aburrida.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Jīntiān de kè hěn wúliáo.

cards.grammarNote: 重要 combines 重 (zhòng, serious/heavy) + 要 (yào, necessary). 最重要的是… = the most important thing is…. Note the pronunciation: zhòng (not chóng) in this compound.
cards.spanishPhrase: La salud es lo más importante.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Jiànkāng shì zuì zhòngyào de shì.

cards.grammarNote: 危险 functions as both adjective (很危险 very dangerous) and noun (有危险 there is danger). The noun form is useful: 脱离危险 (out of danger).
cards.spanishPhrase: Ese camino es muy peligroso de noche.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Nà tiáo lù wǎnshang hěn wēixiǎn.

cards.grammarNote: 安全 works as both adjective and noun: 注意安全 (take care / be safe). 安全感 = sense of security. 安全带 = seatbelt.
cards.spanishPhrase: Este barrio es muy seguro.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Zhège xiǎoqū hěn ānquán.

cards.grammarNote: 干净 means clean/tidy. As a result complement it follows a verb: 洗干净了 (washed clean). 打扫干净 (cleaned up thoroughly). 干净 can also describe clear, uncluttered speech or style.
cards.spanishPhrase: El restaurante está muy limpio.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Zhè jiā cāntīng hěn gānjìng.

cards.grammarNote: 脏 (zāng) = dirty/filthy. As a result complement: 弄脏了 (got dirty/soiled). Note: 脏 is also read as zàng in 心脏 (xīnzàng, heart) — different meaning and tone.
cards.spanishPhrase: Mis zapatos están muy sucios.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ de xiézi hěn zāng.

cards.grammarNote: 便宜 (piányí) = cheap in price (positive nuance). Note the irregular pronunciation: 便 is piān (not biàn) in this word. 便宜 can also be a verb meaning 'to take advantage of' in some contexts.
cards.spanishPhrase: La fruta en este mercado es muy barata.
cards.chinesePhrase: 这个市场的水果很便宜。
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Zhège shìchǎng de shuǐguǒ hěn piányí.

cards.grammarNote: 贵 means expensive and is also used in polite address: 贵姓? (May I ask your surname? — lit. 'your honorable surname'). 太贵了 = too expensive. 贵 vs 贵 compounds: 贵重 (precious/valuable).
cards.spanishPhrase: Este restaurante es demasiado caro.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Zhè jiā fàndiàn tài guì le.

cards.grammarNote: 简单 means simple in the sense of uncomplicated. 很简单 = very simple/easy. 简单来说 = to put it simply. Don't confuse with 容易 (easy to do, about effort).
cards.spanishPhrase: La receta es muy simple.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Zhège càipǔ hěn jiǎndān.

cards.grammarNote: 复杂 describes something with many parts or layers. 情况很复杂 (the situation is complicated). It is the antonym of 简单.
cards.spanishPhrase: Esta situación es muy complicada.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Zhège qíngkuàng hěn fùzá.

cards.grammarNote: 正确 is a formal adjective meaning correct or accurate. Colloquially 对 (duì) is used more often: 这样做对吗? 正确 appears in writing and formal speech: 正确答案 (correct answer).
cards.spanishPhrase: Tu respuesta es correcta.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Nǐ de dá'àn shì zhèngquè de.

cards.grammarNote: 错误 is both an adjective (wrong) and a noun (mistake): 犯错误 (make a mistake). As a standalone adjective, 错 (cuò) is more common colloquially: 你说错了 (you said it wrong).
cards.spanishPhrase: Esa información es incorrecta.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Nàgè xìnxī shì cuòwù de.

cards.grammarNote: 奇怪 can be predicate or attributive: 很奇怪 (very strange), 奇怪的事 (strange thing). 觉得奇怪 = to find it strange. 奇 alone means 'odd' (as in odd numbers: 奇数).
cards.spanishPhrase: Su comportamiento me pareció muy raro.
cards.chinesePhrase: 他的行为让我觉得很奇怪。
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Tā de xíngwéi ràng wǒ juéde hěn qíguài.

cards.grammarNote: 有名 = famous/well-known. 著名 (zhùmíng) is more formal and literary. 出名 (chūmíng) emphasizes becoming famous. 有名 is the most neutral everyday choice.
cards.spanishPhrase: Ese cantante es muy famoso en China.
cards.chinesePhrase: 那位歌手在中国很有名。
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Nà wèi gēshǒu zài Zhōngguó hěn yǒumíng.

cards.grammarNote: 普通 means ordinary/average. 普通话 (Pǔtōnghuà) = Mandarin (the 'common language'). 普通人 = ordinary person. 一般 (yībān) is a close synonym, slightly more colloquial.
cards.spanishPhrase: Es un día ordinario sin nada especial.
cards.chinesePhrase: 今天是普通的一天，没什么特别的。
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Jīntiān shì pǔtōng de yī tiān, méi shénme tèbié de.

cards.grammarNote: 真 as an adjective means real or genuine: 真皮 (genuine leather). As an adverb it means 'really': 真好吃! (really delicious!). 真的 adds emphasis: 真的吗? (really?). Contrast with 假 (false/fake).
cards.spanishPhrase: ¿Eso es verdad?
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Nà shì zhēn de ma?

cards.grammarNote: 假 (jiǎ) = fake/false. 假话 (lie), 假装 (pretend). Note: 假 is also read jià (vacation/holiday): 放假 (on holiday), 暑假 (summer vacation) — different meaning and tone.
cards.spanishPhrase: Ese bolso es falso.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Nàgè bāo shì jiǎ de.

cards.grammarNote: 特别 is both an adjective (special: 特别的礼物) and an adverb meaning 'especially/particularly': 我特别喜欢这个 (I especially like this). It is very common in spoken Chinese.
cards.spanishPhrase: Hoy es un día muy especial para mí.
cards.chinesePhrase: 今天对我来说是特别的一天。
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Jīntiān duì wǒ lái shuō shì tèbié de yī tiān.

cards.grammarNote: 忙 is a stative verb/adjective: 我很忙 (I'm busy). 忙着 + verb = busy doing something: 她忙着做饭 (she's busy cooking). 帮忙 (bāngmáng) = to help out.
cards.spanishPhrase: Estoy muy ocupado esta semana.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ zhège xīngqī hěn máng.

cards.grammarNote: 闲 means free/at leisure, the antonym of 忙. 有空 (yǒu kòng) is a more common colloquial phrase for 'to be free/available'. 闲 also implies idle or leisurely: 闲聊 (small talk).
cards.spanishPhrase: Estoy libre esta tarde, podemos salir.
cards.chinesePhrase: 我今天下午有空，我们可以出去。
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ jīntiān xiàwǔ yǒu kòng, wǒmen kěyǐ chūqù.

cards.grammarNote: 非常 is a stronger intensifier than 很. 非常 + adj/verb: 非常好 (extremely good), 非常感谢 (thank you very much). Unlike 很, 非常 cannot be used in comparisons with 比.
cards.spanishPhrase: La comida estuvo muy deliciosa.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Zhè dùn fàn fēicháng hǎochī.

cards.grammarNote: 也 must come before the verb, not at the end of the sentence. 我也喜欢 (I also like it). When two subjects share a predicate: 他也去，我也去 (he's going too, and so am I). Contrast with 还 which adds a new item.
cards.spanishPhrase: Yo también quiero ir.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ yě xiǎng qù.

cards.grammarNote: 都 summarizes a list or plural subject that precedes it: 我们都去 (we all go). It cannot precede the subject. In negation: 都不 (none) vs 不都 (not all — partial negation). 都 comes before the verb.
cards.spanishPhrase: Todos mis amigos hablan chino.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ de péngyǒu dōu huì shuō Zhōngwén.

cards.grammarNote: 只 expresses restriction: 我只吃蔬菜 (I only eat vegetables). 只有 (zhǐyǒu) = only (existential): 只有他知道 (only he knows). 只要 = as long as; 只是 = it's just that.
cards.spanishPhrase: Solo tengo cinco minutos libres.
cards.chinesePhrase: 我只有五分钟空闲时间。
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ zhǐyǒu wǔ fēnzhōng kòngxián shíjiān.

cards.grammarNote: 就 is one of the most versatile adverbs. It signals immediacy (马上就来 — coming right away), logical consequence (如果…就… — if…then…), or emphasis that something is exactly/already so (就是这个 — it's precisely this). Position: always before the verb.
cards.spanishPhrase: Enseguida voy a tu casa.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ mǎshàng jiù qù nǐ jiā.

cards.grammarNote: 还 has two main uses: (1) continuity — 她还在睡 (she's still sleeping); (2) addition — 我要一杯茶，还要一块蛋糕 (I want a tea and also a cake). Don't confuse with 再 (zài), which adds a repeated or future action.
cards.spanishPhrase: Todavía está lloviendo afuera.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wàimiàn hái zài xià yǔ.

cards.grammarNote: 再 refers to a future or anticipated repetition: 再说一遍 (say it again). 又 (yòu) refers to a past or completed repetition: 他又迟到了 (he was late again). Don't confuse 再 and 又.
cards.spanishPhrase: Dilo otra vez, por favor.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Qǐng zài shuō yī biàn.

cards.grammarNote: 已经 marks a completed state or action, often paired with 了: 我已经吃了 (I've already eaten). It appears before the verb. 已经 can also appear before adjectives to mark a changed state: 天已经黑了.
cards.spanishPhrase: Ya terminé mi tarea.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ yǐjīng zuò wán zuòyè le.

cards.grammarNote: 更 is used in comparisons to mean 'even more': 这个更好 (this one is even better). It does NOT stand alone like 'more' in English — a comparison or context is implied. 更加 (gèngjiā) is a more emphatic form.
cards.spanishPhrase: Esta versión es aún mejor.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Zhège bǎnběn gèng hǎo.

cards.grammarNote: 真的 adds emphasis or confirms a claim: 真的很好吃 (it really is delicious), 真的吗？ (really?). As a standalone response it means 'for real' or 'seriously'. Contrast with 真 (zhēn) as an adverb: 真好！ (truly great!)
cards.spanishPhrase: De verdad me gustó mucho la película.
cards.chinesePhrase: 我真的很喜欢那部电影。
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ zhēn de hěn xǐhuān nà bù diànyǐng.

cards.grammarNote: 一定 expresses certainty or determination. 一定会 (will definitely), 一定要 (must/definitely need to). It can also express a set/fixed amount: 一定程度 (a certain degree). The subject precedes 一定.
cards.spanishPhrase: Definitivamente voy a practicar más.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ yīdìng huì duō liànxí de.

cards.grammarNote: 可能 functions as both an adverb (maybe: 他可能来) and a noun (possibility: 有可能 — there's a possibility). 也许 (yěxǔ) is a near synonym but only functions as an adverb.
cards.spanishPhrase: Quizás llegue tarde mañana.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ míngtiān kěnéng huì lái wǎn.

cards.grammarNote: 应该 expresses moral obligation or logical expectation: 你应该道歉 (you should apologize). It is softer than 必须 (bìxū, must). 应该 can also express inference: 他应该在家 (he should be home / I'd expect him to be home).
cards.spanishPhrase: Deberías descansar más.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Nǐ yīnggāi duō xiūxi.

cards.grammarNote: 大概 expresses rough estimation or probability: 大概三点到 (arriving at around 3). As an adjective it means 'approximate': 大概的数字 (approximate figure). 大约 (dàyuē) is a near synonym, slightly more formal.
cards.spanishPhrase: Probablemente llegará a las tres.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Tā dàgài sān diǎn dào.

cards.grammarNote: 差不多 means 'almost the same' or 'more or less': 差不多了 (almost done). It can also mean things are roughly equal: 两个差不多 (the two are about the same). 快…了 (kuài…le) means 'about to' — a different nuance.
cards.spanishPhrase: La cena está casi lista.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǎnfàn chàbuduō zuò hǎo le.

cards.grammarNote: 一起 precedes the verb: 我们一起去吧 (let's go together). 一块儿 (yīkuàir) is a colloquial synonym common in northern Mandarin. 一起 can also mean 'in total': 一起多少钱？ (how much altogether?)
cards.spanishPhrase: Vamos juntos al cine esta noche.
cards.chinesePhrase: 我们今晚一起去看电影吧。
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒmen jīnwǎn yīqǐ qù kàn diànyǐng ba.

cards.grammarNote: 因为 introduces the cause. It is often paired with 所以 (therefore): 因为下雨，所以我没去. In Chinese both clauses can include their conjunction, unlike English where only one is used. 因为 can stand alone without 所以.
cards.spanishPhrase: No fui porque estaba enfermo.
cards.chinesePhrase: 因为我生病了，所以没去。
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Yīnwèi wǒ shēngbìng le, suǒyǐ méi qù.

cards.grammarNote: 所以 introduces the result clause, paired with 因为: 因为…所以…. Used alone it means 'that's why': 所以我没去 (that's why I didn't go). 因此 (yīncǐ) is a more formal written synonym.
cards.spanishPhrase: Llovía, por eso me quedé en casa.
cards.chinesePhrase: 下雨了，所以我待在家里。
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Xià yǔ le, suǒyǐ wǒ dāi zài jiā lǐ.

cards.grammarNote: 虽然 is paired with 但是 or 还是: 虽然很贵，但是很好. Both conjunctions can appear in the same sentence (unlike English). The concession + contrast pair is a fixed structure in Chinese.
cards.spanishPhrase: Aunque está cansado, sigue trabajando.
cards.chinesePhrase: 虽然他很累，但是还在工作。
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Suīrán tā hěn lèi, dànshì hái zài gōngzuò.

cards.grammarNote: 如果…就… is the standard conditional structure: 如果你来，我就很高兴. 如果 can be followed by 的话 (dehuà) for added nuance: 如果你来的话. 要是 (yàoshi) is a colloquial synonym.
cards.spanishPhrase: Si tienes hambre, come algo.
cards.chinesePhrase: 如果你饿了，就吃点东西。
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Rúguǒ nǐ è le, jiù chī diǎn dōngxi.

cards.grammarNote: 或者 (huòzhě) connects alternatives in statements: 你可以喝茶或者喝咖啡. 还是 (háishi) is used in questions: 你要茶还是咖啡？ Never swap these: 还是 in questions, 或者 in statements.
cards.spanishPhrase: Puedes beber té o café.
cards.chinesePhrase: 你可以喝茶或者喝咖啡。
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Nǐ kěyǐ hē chá huòzhě hē kāfēi.

cards.grammarNote: 而且 adds an additional, reinforcing point: 他聪明，而且努力 (he's smart and also hardworking). It often follows 不但/不仅 (not only…): 不但快，而且便宜 (not only fast but also cheap).
cards.spanishPhrase: Es inteligente y además muy trabajador.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Tā hěn cōngmíng, érqiě hěn nǔlì.

cards.grammarNote: 不过 is a softer contrast than 但是, often used in spoken Chinese to introduce a mild objection or qualifier: 这个不错，不过有点贵 (this is good, but a bit expensive). It can also mean 'only/just': 不过如此 (just like that, nothing special).
cards.spanishPhrase: La comida es buena, sin embargo es cara.
cards.chinesePhrase: 这个菜不错，不过有点贵。
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Zhège cài búcuò, búguò yǒudiǎn guì.

cards.grammarNote: 可是 is interchangeable with 但是 in most spoken contexts. It is slightly more colloquial and often carries a tone of mild complaint or disappointment. 但是 is more neutral and common in writing.
cards.spanishPhrase: Quiero ir, pero estoy muy cansado.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ xiǎng qù, kěshì wǒ tài lèi le.

cards.grammarNote: 不仅 is the formal pair to 而且/也: 不仅漂亮，而且聪明 (not only beautiful but also smart). 不但 (bùdàn) is the more colloquial equivalent. Both introduce the first of two reinforcing clauses.
cards.spanishPhrase: No solo habla chino, sino también inglés.
cards.chinesePhrase: 她不仅会说中文，也会说英语。
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Tā bùjǐn huì shuō Zhōngwén, yě huì shuō Yīngyǔ.

cards.grammarNote: 而是 follows a negation to introduce the actual alternative: 不是A，而是B (not A but rather B). It is the corrective counterpart of 而且. 不是…而是… is a fixed contrast pattern.
cards.spanishPhrase: No es fácil, sino muy difícil.
cards.chinesePhrase: 不是容易的，而是很难的。
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Bú shì róngyì de, ér shì hěn nán de.

cards.grammarNote: 然后 sequences events: 先…然后… (first…then…). It begins a clause and is not placed mid-clause. 接着 (jiēzhe) implies tighter temporal connection; 以后 (yǐhòu) means 'after that' in a broader sense.
cards.spanishPhrase: Primero como y luego salgo.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ xiān chīfàn, ránhòu chūmén.

cards.grammarNote: 既然 introduces a conceded fact and is paired with 就: 既然你来了，就留下来吃饭吧 (since you're here, stay for dinner). It is more assertive than 因为 and implies the speaker accepts the premise.
cards.spanishPhrase: Ya que estás aquí, quédate a cenar.
cards.chinesePhrase: 既然你来了，就留下来吃饭吧。
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Jìrán nǐ lái le, jiù liú xiàlái chīfàn ba.

cards.spanish: siempre que / con tal de que
cards.grammarNote: 只要…就… = as long as…then…: 只要努力，就会成功 (as long as you work hard, you'll succeed). 只有…才… (zhǐyǒu…cái…) = only if…then…, which is more restrictive.
cards.spanishPhrase: Siempre que estudies, aprobarás el examen.
cards.chinesePhrase: 只要你学习，就会通过考试。
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Zhǐyào nǐ xuéxí, jiù huì tōngguò kǎoshì.

cards.grammarNote: 除非…才/否则… = unless… (only then / otherwise): 除非你道歉，否则我不原谅你 (unless you apologize, I won't forgive you). 除非 and 只有 are related but 除非 foregrounds the exception.
cards.spanishPhrase: No saldré a menos que pare de llover.
cards.chinesePhrase: 除非雨停了，否则我不出去。
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Chúfēi yǔ tíng le, fǒuzé wǒ bù chūqù.

cards.grammarNote: 即使…也… = even if…still…: 即使很难，我也要试试 (even if it's hard, I still want to try). 即使 presents a hypothetical extreme; 虽然 presents a real conceded fact. They are not interchangeable.
cards.spanishPhrase: Aunque sea difícil, lo intentaré.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Jíshǐ hěn nán, wǒ yě yào shìshì.

cards.grammarNote: 对 as a coverb introduces the target or topic: 对我来说 (for me / in my case), 对你好 (good for you), 他对我很好 (he treats me well). 对 comes before the object, which precedes the main verb.
cards.spanishPhrase: Eso es muy bueno para tu salud.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Nà duì nǐ de jiànkāng hěn hǎo.

cards.grammarNote: 从 marks the starting point of movement or time: 从北京来 (from Beijing), 从明天开始 (starting from tomorrow). It precedes the origin, which precedes the verb. Contrast 离 (lí, distance from a fixed point) and 从 (starting point of movement).
cards.spanishPhrase: Él viene desde Beijing.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Tā cóng Běijīng lái.

cards.grammarNote: 到 marks the endpoint of movement or time: 从北京到上海 (from Beijing to Shanghai), 从早到晚 (from morning to night). As a result complement after a verb, it signals reaching a goal: 找到了 (found it), 做到了 (achieved it).
cards.spanishPhrase: Caminaremos hasta la escuela juntos.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒmen yīqǐ zǒu dào xuéxiào.

cards.grammarNote: 为 (wèi) introduces a beneficiary or purpose: 为你做的 (done for you). 为了 (wèile) more explicitly marks purpose: 为了健康而运动. Note: 为 (wéi) with 2nd tone = to be/act as — a different word.
cards.spanishPhrase: Lo hice para ti.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Zhè shì wèi nǐ zuò de.

cards.grammarNote: 比 is the standard comparison marker: A比B + adj (A is more adj than B): 他比我高 (he is taller than me). The adjective appears without 很 in this structure. Negation uses 没有: A没有B那么高 (A is not as tall as B). Don't use 更 with 比 together (redundant).
cards.spanishPhrase: Él es más alto que yo.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Tā bǐ wǒ gāo.

cards.grammarNote: 把 is a disposal marker that moves a definite, affected object before the verb: 我把书放在桌上 (I put the book on the table). The verb must be transitive, resultative, or directional — never a bare verb alone after 把. 把 cannot be used with stative verbs or negated with 不 in most cases.
cards.spanishPhrase: Puse el libro sobre la mesa.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ bǎ shū fàng zài zhuōzi shàng.

cards.grammarNote: 被 introduces the agent in a passive construction: 书被他拿走了 (the book was taken by him). In Chinese, 被 passives often carry an undesirable or unexpected nuance. The agent may be omitted: 钱被偷了 (the money was stolen). Contrast with 把 (active disposal).
cards.spanishPhrase: Mi dinero fue robado.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ de qián bèi tōu le.

cards.grammarNote: 向 indicates direction or orientation: 向左转 (turn left), 向老师学习 (learn from the teacher). It is more literary than 往 (wǎng, toward), though both can mark direction. 向 also expresses addressing someone: 向…道歉 (apologize to…).
cards.spanishPhrase: Gira hacia la izquierda en la esquina.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Zài lùkǒu xiàng zuǒ zhuǎn.

cards.grammarNote: 离 expresses spatial or temporal distance from a fixed reference point: 学校离这里很近 (the school is close to here). 离 cannot mark the source of motion — use 从 for that. 离 + time expression: 离开学还有两天 (two days until school starts).
cards.spanishPhrase: La escuela está lejos de aquí.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Xuéxiào lí zhèlǐ hěn yuǎn.

cards.grammarNote: 除了 has two meanings depending on the rest of the sentence: (1) except: 除了他，大家都来了 (everyone came except him) — paired with 都; (2) besides/in addition to: 除了中文，她还会法语 (besides Chinese, she also speaks French) — paired with 还.
cards.spanishPhrase: Además de chino, ella habla francés.
cards.chinesePhrase: 除了中文，她还会说法语。
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Chúle Zhōngwén, tā hái huì shuō Fǎyǔ.

cards.grammarNote: 关于 introduces a topic or subject matter: 关于这个问题 (regarding this issue). It typically appears at the start of a sentence or clause. 对于 (duìyú) is a near synonym but focuses more on a perspective or attitude: 对于这件事，我有不同的看法.
cards.spanishPhrase: Tengo una pregunta sobre este tema.
cards.chinesePhrase: 关于这个问题，我有一个问题。
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Guānyú zhège wèntí, wǒ yǒu yī gè wèntí.

cards.grammarNote: 根据 precedes the evidence or basis: 根据报告 (according to the report), 根据我的经验 (based on my experience). It can also function as a verb meaning 'to be based on'. 按照 (ànzhào) emphasizes following rules or standards.
cards.spanishPhrase: Según el informe, el tiempo mejorará.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Gēnjù bàogào, tiānqì huì biàn hǎo.

cards.grammarNote: 通过 as a preposition means 'through/by means of': 通过努力实现目标 (achieve goals through hard work). As a verb it means to pass (an exam, a law): 通过考试 (pass an exam), 法案已经通过 (the bill has passed).
cards.spanishPhrase: Aprendí inglés mediante el esfuerzo.
cards.chinesePhrase: 我通过努力学会了英语。
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ tōngguò nǔlì xuéhuì le Yīngyǔ.

cards.grammarNote: 按照 stresses following a rule, plan, or standard: 按照规定 (in accordance with the regulations), 按照计划 (as planned). 根据 focuses on information as the basis; 按照 focuses on adherence to a norm or procedure.
cards.spanishPhrase: Todo se hará conforme al plan.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Yīqiè ànzhào jìhuà jìnxíng.

cards.grammarNote: 颜色 (yánsè) is the general word for 'color'. To ask about color: 这是什么颜色？(What color is this?) or 你喜欢什么颜色？(What color do you like?)
cards.spanishPhrase: ¿Cuál es tu color favorito?
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Nǐ zuì xǐhuān shénme yánsè?

cards.grammarNote: Color words in Chinese are often formed with 色 (sè, color) as a suffix: 红色, 蓝色, etc. In everyday speech 色 is sometimes dropped when the color directly modifies a noun: 红苹果 (red apple).
cards.spanishPhrase: Ella lleva una camisa roja hoy.
cards.chinesePhrase: 她今天穿了一件红色的衬衫。
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Tā jīntiān chuān le yī jiàn hóngsè de chènshān.

cards.spanishPhrase: Me gusta el jugo de naranja frío.
cards.chinesePhrase: 我喜欢喝冷的橙色果汁。
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ xǐhuān hē lěng de chéngsè guǒzhī.
cards.grammarNote:
橙色 (chéngsè) combines 橙 (chéng, orange the fruit) + 色 (sè, color), literally 'orange-fruit color.' Most color terms in Chinese can appear with or without 色: 橙色 and 橙 alone; however, 色 is typically required when the color modifies a noun attributively or stands alone as a noun.
The pattern Noun + 色 + 的 + Noun (e.g., 橙色的果汁, orange-colored juice) adds 的 as the attributive marker.
色 (sè) also means 'form/color/appearance' in Buddhist terminology.

cards.spanishPhrase: Las flores amarillas son muy bonitas.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Huángsè de huā hěn piàoliang.
cards.grammarNote:
黄色 (huángsè) pairs 黄 (huáng, yellow) + 色 (sè, color).
Yellow holds deep imperial significance in Chinese culture—the emperor's robes were yellow, and the legendary first emperor is called 黄帝 (Huángdì, the Yellow Emperor).
When used attributively, the pattern is 黄色的 + noun: 黄色的花 (huángsè de huā, yellow flowers).
In colloquial speech, 黄色 has also developed the meaning 'pornographic/adult content'—a euphemism parallel to English 'blue.' Context usually makes the intended meaning clear, but be aware of this secondary meaning.
Note the ü vowel in 绿 (lǜ). Chinese also uses 青 (qīng) for a blue-green shade, often seen in classical texts and color names like 青天 (blue sky) or 青草 (green grass).
cards.spanishPhrase: El semáforo está en verde, podemos pasar.
cards.chinesePhrase: 灯是绿色的，我们可以走了。
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Dēng shì lǜsè de, wǒmen kěyǐ zǒu le.

cards.spanishPhrase: El cielo es de color azul hoy.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Jīntiān tiānkōng shì lánsè de.
cards.grammarNote:
蓝色 (lánsè) combines 蓝 (lán, blue) + 色 (sè, color).
Interestingly, classical Chinese used 青 (qīng) to cover both blue and green, and this usage persists in compounds: 青蛙 (qīngwā, frog, literally 'blue-green frog'), 青天 (qīngtiān, blue sky).
Modern Mandarin has differentiated 蓝 (blue) from 绿 (lǜ, green), but 青 survives in many fixed expressions.
The sky (天空 tiānkōng) uses 空 (kōng, empty/sky) to specify the atmospheric expanse rather than 天 alone.

cards.spanishPhrase: Ella tiene una bolsa de color morado.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Tā yǒu yī gè zǐsè de bāo.
cards.grammarNote:
紫色 (zǐsè) combines 紫 (zǐ, purple) + 色 (sè, color).
Purple was historically a color of high status in China, associated with the Forbidden City (紫禁城 Zǐjìn Chéng, literally 'Purple Forbidden City').
In the example 她有一个紫色的包, 包 (bāo, bag) uses the measure word 个 (gè)—the default classifier for bags and parcels.
紫 also appears in 紫外线 (zǐwàixiàn, ultraviolet rays), literally 'outside-purple rays,' because UV lies beyond the visible violet end of the spectrum.

cards.spanishPhrase: Él siempre usa ropa de color blanco.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Tā zǒngshì chuān báisè de yīfu.
cards.grammarNote:
白色 (báisè) pairs 白 (bái, white) + 色 (sè, color).
In Chinese culture, white is associated with mourning and funerals—the opposite of Western wedding connotations—so gifting white flowers or wearing all white to celebrations is considered inauspicious.
The character 白 is a pictograph of a candle flame or the sun at its brightest.
The example 他总是穿白色的衣服 uses 总是 (zǒngshì, always) and 穿 (chuān, to wear clothing)—distinguished from 戴 (dài, to wear accessories like hats, glasses, jewelry).

cards.spanishPhrase: Tengo un gato de color negro.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ yǒu yī zhī hēisè de māo.
cards.grammarNote:
黑色 (hēisè) combines 黑 (hēi, black) + 色 (sè, color).
黑 pictographically depicts fire smoke and soot, conveying darkness through blackening.
In the example 我有一只黑色的猫, the measure word for cats (and most small animals) is 只 (zhī), not 个.
只 (zhī) is the standard classifier for small animals: 一只猫 (yī zhī māo, one cat), 一只鸟 (yī zhī niǎo, one bird).
黑 also appears idiomatically: 黑市 (hēishì, black market), 黑白 (hēibái, black and white/right and wrong).

cards.spanishPhrase: El cielo gris indica que lloverá.
cards.chinesePhrase: 灰色的天空说明要下雨了。
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Huīsè de tiānkōng shuōmíng yào xià yǔ le.
cards.grammarNote:
灰色 (huīsè) combines 灰 (huī, ash/gray) + 色 (sè, color).
The character 灰 depicts a hand (又 yòu) over fire (火 huǒ), evoking the ash left after burning—a concrete etymology for the color of ashes.
In the example 灰色的天空说明要下雨了, 说明 (shuōmíng, to indicate/explain/show) functions as a verb meaning 'to signal that.' The pattern 要……了 (yào…le) expresses imminent action: 要下雨了 (yào xià yǔ le, it's about to rain).
灰色 also carries a metaphorical meaning of ambiguity: 灰色地带 (huīsè dìdài, gray area).
粉红色 (fěnhóngsè) literally means 'powder-red color'. It is often shortened to 粉色 (fěnsè) in casual speech.
cards.spanishPhrase: A mi hermana le gustan las flores rosadas.
cards.chinesePhrase: 我妹妹喜欢粉红色的花。
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ mèimei xǐhuān fěnhóngsè de huā.

cards.grammarNote: 棕色 (zōngsè) literally means 'palm-tree color', referencing the brownish fibers of a palm tree. 咖啡色 (kāfēisè, coffee color) is a common synonym.
cards.spanishPhrase: Él compró unos zapatos de color marrón.
cards.chinesePhrase: 他买了一双棕色的鞋子。
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Tā mǎi le yī shuāng zōngsè de xiézi.

cards.grammarNote: 金色 (jīnsè) refers to the color gold. 金 (jīn) also means gold as a metal or money in other contexts, e.g., 黄金 (huángjīn, gold bullion).
cards.spanishPhrase: El sol de la tarde se ve dorado.
cards.chinesePhrase: 下午的太阳看起来是金色的。
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Xiàwǔ de tàiyáng kàn qǐlái shì jīnsè de.

cards.grammarNote: 家庭 (jiātíng) refers to family as a unit or institution. 家 (jiā) alone can mean both 'family' and 'home/house' in everyday speech: 我家 = my family / my home.
cards.spanishPhrase: Mi familia es muy importante para mí.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ de jiātíng duì wǒ hěn zhòngyào.

cards.grammarNote: 父母 (fùmǔ) is a compound of 父 (fù, father) and 母 (mǔ, mother). Chinese has entirely separate terms for paternal vs. maternal relatives — a key feature of the family vocabulary system. Measure word: 位 (wèi) for people (polite).
cards.spanishPhrase: Mis padres viven en otra ciudad.
cards.chinesePhrase: 我的父母住在另一个城市。
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ de fùmǔ zhù zài lìng yī gè chéngshì.

cards.grammarNote: 爸爸 (bàba) is the informal word for father. The formal term is 父亲 (fùqīn). When addressing or referring to someone else's father, use 爸爸 or 父亲 in context; 令尊 (lìngzūn) is a very formal honorific for another's father.
cards.spanishPhrase: Mi papá cocina muy bien.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ bàba zuò fàn hěn hǎo chī.

cards.grammarNote: 妈妈 (māma) is the informal word for mother. The formal term is 母亲 (mǔqīn). Chinese kinship terms strictly distinguish paternal and maternal lines — the word for mother's mother (外婆) is completely different from father's mother (奶奶).
cards.spanishPhrase: Mi mamá me llama todos los días.
cards.chinesePhrase: 我妈妈每天给我打电话。
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ māma měi tiān gěi wǒ dǎ diànhuà.

cards.grammarNote: Chinese distinguishes older and younger siblings with separate words. 哥哥 (gēge) = older brother; 弟弟 (dìdi) = younger brother. There is no single word equivalent to English 'brother' that covers both ages — you must specify. Measure word: 个 (gè) or 位 (wèi, polite).
cards.spanishPhrase: Mi hermano mayor trabaja en Madrid.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ gēge zài Běijīng gōngzuò.

cards.grammarNote: 弟弟 (dìdi) = younger brother. To say 'I have a younger brother': 我有一个弟弟. Note the tonal reduplication: both syllables are 4th tone but the second is often said with a neutral tone in natural speech.
cards.spanishPhrase: Mi hermano menor va al colegio.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ dìdi hái zài shàng xué.

cards.grammarNote: 姐姐 (jiějie) = older sister. Like siblings in general, older and younger sisters have entirely separate words in Chinese. 姐 alone is used in compounds, e.g., 大姐 (eldest sister), 表姐 (older female cousin on mother's side).
cards.spanishPhrase: Mi hermana mayor es muy lista.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ jiějie fēicháng cōngming.

cards.grammarNote: 妹妹 (mèimei) = younger sister. The second syllable is typically pronounced with a neutral tone in natural speech. Cousins are also classified by age and side of family: 表妹 = younger female cousin (maternal), 堂妹 = younger female cousin (paternal).
cards.spanishPhrase: Mi hermana menor tiene cinco años.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ mèimei cái wǔ suì.

cards.grammarNote: 爷爷 (yéye) is specifically the father's father. The mother's father is 外公 (wàigōng) — a completely different word. This strict paternal/maternal distinction applies throughout Chinese kinship terminology. Measure word: 位 (wèi, polite).
cards.spanishPhrase: Mi abuelo paterno era maestro.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ yéye yǐqián shì lǎoshī.

cards.grammarNote: 奶奶 (nǎinai) is specifically the father's mother. The mother's mother is 外婆 (wàipó). The prefix 外 (wài, outside/external) traditionally marks maternal relatives, reflecting the historical patrilineal family structure.
cards.spanishPhrase: Mi abuela paterna nos hace empanadas.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ nǎinai gěi wǒmen zuò jiǎozi.

cards.grammarNote: 外公 (wàigōng) is the mother's father. 外 (wài) literally means 'outside', historically signifying the maternal side of the family. In Taiwan and some regions, 外祖父 (wàizǔfù) is the more formal term. Measure word: 位 (wèi, polite).
cards.spanishPhrase: Mi abuelo materno vive en el campo.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ wàigōng zhù zài nóngcūn.

cards.grammarNote: 外婆 (wàipó) is the mother's mother. Together, the maternal grandparents are 外祖父母 (wàizǔfùmǔ) in formal language. In northern China, 姥姥 (lǎolao) and 姥爷 (lǎoyé) are regional alternatives for maternal grandmother and grandfather.
cards.spanishPhrase: Mi abuela materna cumple ochenta años.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ wàipó jīnnián bāshí suì le.

cards.grammarNote: 丈夫 (zhàngfu) is the standard word for husband. Colloquially, 老公 (lǎogōng) is widely used between spouses. 先生 (xiānsheng) can also mean husband in some contexts, though it more commonly means 'Mr.' or 'teacher'.
cards.spanishPhrase: Su esposo trabaja en el hospital.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Tā zhàngfu zài yīyuàn gōngzuò.

cards.grammarNote: 妻子 (qīzi) is the standard word for wife. Colloquially, 老婆 (lǎopó) is the affectionate informal term. Note: 子 (zi) here is an unstressed suffix, not the word for 'son' or 'child'.
cards.spanishPhrase: Su esposa habla tres idiomas.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Tā qīzi huì shuō sān zhǒng yǔyán.

cards.grammarNote: 儿子 (érzi) = son. 儿 (ér) means 'child/son' and appears in many compounds: 女儿 (daughter), 儿童 (children in general). Measure word: 个 (gè).
cards.spanishPhrase: Su hijo estudia medicina.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Tā de érzi zài xué yī.

cards.grammarNote: 女儿 (nǚ'ér) = daughter. 女 (nǚ) means female/woman. The apostrophe in pinyin separates syllables where ambiguity could arise: nǚ'ér vs. nǚér. Measure word: 个 (gè).
cards.spanishPhrase: Su hija toca el piano muy bien.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Tā nǚ'ér gāngqín tán de hěn hǎo.

cards.grammarNote: 孩子 (háizi) can refer to one child or children in general — Chinese nouns don't inflect for plural. To specify multiple children: 孩子们 (háizimen) or 几个孩子 (jǐ gè háizi, several children). Measure word: 个 (gè).
cards.spanishPhrase: Los niños juegan en el parque.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Háizimen zài gōngyuán lǐ wán.

cards.grammarNote: 婴儿 (yīng'ér) is the formal/standard word for baby or infant. The colloquial term 宝宝 (bǎobao) is widely used by parents and is more affectionate. Measure word: 个 (gè).
cards.spanishPhrase: El bebé duerme todo el día.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Yīng'ér zhěng tiān dōu zài shuìjiào.

cards.grammarNote: 老师 (lǎoshī) is the common word for teacher at all levels. It is also used as a respectful form of address: 王老师 (Teacher Wang). 教授 (jiàoshòu) specifically means professor at a university. Measure word: 位 (wèi, polite) or 个 (gè).
cards.spanishPhrase: El maestro explica la lección con cuidado.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Lǎoshī rènzhēn de jiǎngkè.

cards.grammarNote: 课 (kè) means a class period or lesson. 上课 (shàngkè) = to attend class / class is in session; 下课 (xiàkè) = class is over. Measure word: 节 (jié) for individual class periods; 门 (mén) for subjects/courses.
cards.spanishPhrase: La clase de chino empieza a las nueve.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Zhōngwén kè jiǔ diǎn kāishǐ.

cards.grammarNote: 课本 (kèběn) = textbook (lit. 'lesson book'). 教材 (jiàocái) is a slightly broader term meaning 'teaching materials' or 'coursebook'. Measure word: 本 (běn) for books.
cards.spanishPhrase: Olvidé mi libro de texto en casa.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ bǎ kèběn wàng zài jiālǐ le.

cards.grammarNote: 笔 (bǐ) is the general word for pen or writing instrument. Types: 钢笔 (gāngbǐ, fountain pen), 圆珠笔 (yuánzhūbǐ, ballpoint pen), 毛笔 (máobǐ, calligraphy brush). Measure word: 支 (zhī) or 根 (gēn).
cards.spanishPhrase: ¿Me puedes prestar tu bolígrafo?
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Nǐ néng jiè wǒ nǐ de bǐ ma?

cards.grammarNote: 铅笔 (qiānbǐ) literally means 'lead pen'. 铅 (qiān) = lead (the metal). Measure word: 支 (zhī) or 根 (gēn).
cards.spanishPhrase: Necesito un lápiz para dibujar.
cards.chinesePhrase: 我需要一支铅笔来画画。
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ xūyào yī zhī qiānbǐ lái huà huà.

cards.grammarNote: 纸 (zhǐ) = paper. Measure word: 张 (zhāng) for flat sheets — this is the standard measure word for paper, tickets, photos, and other flat items. 一张纸 = one sheet of paper.
cards.spanishPhrase: Dame una hoja de papel, por favor.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Qǐng gěi wǒ yī zhāng zhǐ.

cards.grammarNote: 作业 (zuòyè) means homework or assignment. 做作业 (zuò zuòyè) = to do homework. Note: 做 (zuò, to do) and the first character of 作业 (zuò) are different characters despite the same pronunciation and tone.
cards.spanishPhrase: Tengo que terminar mi tarea hoy.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ jīntiān bìxū zuò wán zuòyè.

cards.grammarNote: 考试 (kǎoshì) functions as both a noun (exam) and a verb (to take an exam): 我要考试 = I have an exam / I'm going to take an exam. 参加考试 = to participate in/take an exam (more formal). Measure word: 次 (cì) for instances.
cards.spanishPhrase: Tengo un examen mañana por la mañana.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ míngtiān shàngwǔ yǒu kǎoshì.

cards.grammarNote: 成绩 (chéngjì) means academic grades or results. 分数 (fēnshù) specifically means numerical score. 成绩单 (chéngjìdān) = report card / transcript. Measure word: 个 (gè) for individual grades.
cards.spanishPhrase: Saqué una buena nota en el examen.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ zhè cì kǎoshì chéngjì hěn hǎo.

cards.grammarNote: 知识 (zhīshi) = knowledge, learning. The second syllable 识 is pronounced in neutral tone (shi, not shí) in this compound. 知识分子 (zhīshi fènzǐ) = intellectual. Compare: 知道 (zhīdào) = to know (a fact).
cards.spanishPhrase: Leer libros nos da mucho conocimiento.
cards.chinesePhrase: 读书能给我们很多知识。
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Dú shū néng gěi wǒmen hěn duō zhīshi.

cards.grammarNote: 大学生 (dàxuéshēng) = college/university student. Built from 大学 (dàxué, university) + 生 (shēng, person/student). Compare: 中学生 (zhōngxuéshēng, middle/high school student), 小学生 (xiǎoxuéshēng, elementary school student). Measure word: 个 (gè) or 位 (wèi, polite).
cards.spanishPhrase: Mi hermano es estudiante universitario ahora.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ gēge xiànzài shì dàxuéshēng.

cards.grammarNote: 班级 (bānjí) refers to a class as a group of students. 班 (bān) alone is commonly used: 我们班 (our class). 年级 (niánjí) means grade level (e.g., 三年级 = 3rd grade). Measure word: 个 (gè).
cards.spanishPhrase: Nuestra clase tiene treinta estudiantes.
cards.chinesePhrase: 我们班级有三十个学生。
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒmen bānjí yǒu sānshí gè xuéshēng.

cards.grammarNote: 药 (yào) = medicine/drug in general. 吃药 (chī yào) = to take medicine (lit. 'eat medicine'). 中药 (zhōngyào) = traditional Chinese medicine; 西药 (xīyào) = Western medicine. Measure word: 粒 (lì) for pills/tablets; 瓶 (píng) for bottles.
cards.spanishPhrase: El médico me dio medicina para la tos.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Yīshēng gěi le wǒ zhǐké de yào.

cards.grammarNote: 病 (bìng) is both a noun (illness) and a verb (to be sick): 我病了 (I'm sick). 生病 (shēng bìng) = to fall ill. 病人 (bìngrén) = patient (sick person). 疾病 (jíbìng) is a more formal compound meaning disease.
cards.spanishPhrase: Está en cama porque tiene una enfermedad.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Tā yīnwèi shēng bìng tǎng zài chuáng shàng.

cards.grammarNote: 疼 (téng) = to hurt / to ache. Used as a verb or adjective: 我的腿疼 (My leg hurts). 疼痛 (téngtòng) is a more formal compound noun for pain. 痛 (tòng) is a near-synonym, often interchangeable, but 痛 can also mean intense pain.
cards.spanishPhrase: Me duele mucho la pierna derecha.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ de yòu tuǐ hěn téng.

cards.grammarNote: 头疼 (tóuténg) = headache (lit. 'head hurts'). Also written/said as 头痛 (tóutòng). This pattern extends to other body parts: 肚子疼 (dùzi téng, stomachache), 牙疼 (yáténg, toothache). Both adjective and verb: 我头疼 = I have a headache.
cards.spanishPhrase: Tengo un fuerte dolor de cabeza hoy.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ jīntiān tóuténg de hěn lìhai.

cards.grammarNote: 发烧 (fāshāo) = to have a fever (lit. 'to emit burning heat'). Functions as a verb-object compound: 我发烧了 (I have/got a fever). 量体温 (liáng tǐwēn) = to take one's temperature. 退烧 (tuìshāo) = for a fever to go down.
cards.spanishPhrase: El niño tiene fiebre y necesita descansar.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Háizi fāshāo le, xūyào xiūxi.

cards.grammarNote: 咳嗽 (késou) = to cough / a cough. The second syllable 嗽 (sou) is often pronounced in neutral tone. Functions as a verb: 他一直咳嗽 (He keeps coughing). 止咳 (zhǐké) = to suppress a cough.
cards.spanishPhrase: Lleva tres días tosiendo sin parar.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Tā késou yǐjīng sān tiān le.

cards.grammarNote: 感冒 (gǎnmào) = the common cold; also used as a verb: 我感冒了 (I have a cold / I caught a cold). Do not confuse with 着凉 (zháoliáng, to catch a chill) or 流感 (liúgǎn, influenza/flu).
cards.spanishPhrase: Me resfrié por la lluvia de ayer.
cards.chinesePhrase: 我因为昨天下雨感冒了。
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ yīnwèi zuótiān xià yǔ gǎnmào le.

cards.grammarNote: 过敏 (guòmǐn) = allergy; also used as a verb: 我对花粉过敏 (I'm allergic to pollen). 过敏反应 (guòmǐn fǎnyìng) = allergic reaction. 食物过敏 (shíwù guòmǐn) = food allergy.
cards.spanishPhrase: Soy alérgico al polen en primavera.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ chūntiān duì huāfěn guòmǐn.

cards.grammarNote: 手术 (shǒushù) = surgery (lit. 'hand technique'). 做手术 (zuò shǒushù) = to perform/have surgery. 手术室 (shǒushùshì) = operating room. 外科手术 (wàikē shǒushù) = surgical operation. Measure word: 次 (cì) for times/instances.
cards.spanishPhrase: El médico dijo que necesita una cirugía.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Yīshēng shuō tā xūyào zuò shǒushù.

cards.grammarNote: 急救 (jíjiù) = first aid / emergency rescue (lit. 'urgent rescue'). 急救箱 (jíjiùxiāng) = first aid kit. 急救车 (jíjiùchē) is another word for ambulance alongside 救护车. 拨打急救电话 = to call emergency services.
cards.spanishPhrase: Aprendimos primeros auxilios en la escuela.
cards.chinesePhrase: 我们在学校学了急救知识。
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒmen zài xuéxiào xué le jíjiù zhīshi.

cards.grammarNote: 救护车 (jiùhùchē) = ambulance (lit. 'rescue-protect vehicle'). 叫救护车 (jiào jiùhùchē) = to call an ambulance. In China, the emergency number for an ambulance is 120. Measure word: 辆 (liàng) for vehicles.
cards.spanishPhrase: Llama a la ambulancia, es una emergencia.
cards.chinesePhrase: 快叫救护车，是紧急情况。
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Kuài jiào jiùhùchē, shì jǐnjí qíngkuàng.

cards.grammarNote: 体温 (tǐwēn) = body temperature. 量体温 (liáng tǐwēn) = to take/measure one's temperature. 体温计 (tǐwēnjì) = thermometer. Normal body temperature in Chinese medical context is typically stated as 36–37°C.
cards.spanishPhrase: El médico midió mi temperatura corporal.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Yīshēng bāng wǒ liáng le tǐwēn.

cards.grammarNote: 上 (shàng) = up, above, on top. As a directional complement it indicates upward movement: 走上去 (walk up). In location expressions: 桌子上 (on the table). 上面 (shàngmiàn) = the top side/surface. Also means 'previous' in time: 上个月 (last month).
cards.spanishPhrase: El avión vuela arriba de las nubes.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Fēijī zài yún de shàngmiàn fēi.

cards.grammarNote: 下 (xià) = down, below, under. 桌子下 (under the table). As a complement: 走下去 (walk down). Also means 'next' in time: 下个月 (next month). 下面 (xiàmiàn) = the bottom side.
cards.spanishPhrase: El gato está debajo de la mesa.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Māo zài zhuōzi xiàmiàn.

cards.grammarNote: 左 (zuǒ) = left (direction). 左边 (zuǒbiān) or 左面 (zuǒmiàn) = the left side. 向左转 (xiàng zuǒ zhuǎn) = turn left. In Chinese culture, 左 has historically been considered either auspicious or inferior depending on the era and context.
cards.spanishPhrase: El banco está a la izquierda del parque.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Yínháng zài gōngyuán de zuǒbiān.

cards.grammarNote: 右 (yòu) = right (direction). 右边 (yòubiān) = the right side. 向右转 (xiàng yòu zhuǎn) = turn right. When giving directions in Chinese, 左边 and 右边 are far more common than bare 左 or 右.
cards.spanishPhrase: Gira a la derecha en la próxima calle.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Zài xià yī tiáo jiē xiàng yòu zhuǎn.

cards.grammarNote: 前 (qián) = front, ahead, forward. 前面 (qiánmiàn) / 前边 (qiánbiān) = in front. 前 also indicates past time: 以前 (yǐqián, before/in the past). 向前走 = walk forward.
cards.spanishPhrase: La tienda está justo adelante de ti.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Shāngdiàn jiù zài nǐ qiánmiàn.

cards.grammarNote: 后 (hòu) = back, behind, after. 后面 (hòumiàn) / 后边 (hòubiān) = behind / at the back. 后 also indicates future time: 以后 (yǐhòu, afterwards/in the future). 后来 (hòulái) = later on.
cards.spanishPhrase: El jardín está detrás de la casa.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Huāyuán zài fángzi hòumiàn.

cards.grammarNote: 里 (lǐ) = inside, within. 里面 (lǐmiàn) / 里边 (lǐbiān) = the inside. 房间里 (in the room). Note: 里 (lǐ) meaning 'inside' is different from 里 (lǐ) the unit of distance (Chinese mile ≈ 500m), which is written the same but in a different context.
cards.spanishPhrase: El gato está dentro de la casa.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Māo zài fángjiān lǐ.

cards.grammarNote: 外 (wài) = outside, external. 外面 (wàimiàn) / 外边 (wàibiān) = the outside. 外 also means 'foreign' in many compounds: 外国 (wàiguó, foreign country), 外语 (wàiyǔ, foreign language). Recall: 外婆/外公 use 外 to mark the maternal line.
cards.spanishPhrase: Los niños juegan afuera.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Háizimen zài wàimiàn wán.

cards.grammarNote: 中间 (zhōngjiān) = middle, center, between. 在…中间 = in the middle of / between: 在两棵树中间 (between two trees). Note: 中 (zhōng) = middle/China; 中国 (Zhōngguó) = China, lit. 'Middle Kingdom'.
cards.spanishPhrase: El parque está en el centro.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Gōngyuán zài zhōngjiān.

cards.grammarNote: 对面 (duìmiàn) = opposite, across from, facing. 银行的对面 (across from the bank). 对 (duì) means 'facing/towards' and also 'correct', so context is key. 正对面 (zhèng duìmiàn) = directly opposite.
cards.spanishPhrase: El banco está enfrente de la tienda.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Yínháng zài shāngdiàn de duìmiàn.

cards.grammarNote: 件 (jiàn) is the typical measure word: 一件事情 (one matter/thing). Often shortened to 事 in speech.
cards.spanishPhrase: Tengo muchas cosas que hacer hoy.
cards.chinesePhrase: 我今天有很多事情要做。
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ jīntiān yǒu hěn duō shìqing yào zuò.

cards.grammarNote: Refers to time as a concept or duration. 时候 (shíhou) means 'the time when / moment'. 没有时间 = 'no time'.
cards.spanishPhrase: No tengo tiempo ahora mismo.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ xiànzài méiyǒu shíjiān.

cards.grammarNote: 个 (gè) is used as the measure word: 一个问题 (one question/problem). 没问题 = 'no problem'.
cards.spanishPhrase: Ese es un problema muy difícil.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Zhège wèntí hěn nán jiějué.

cards.grammarNote: 种 (zhǒng) is a common measure word: 一种方法 (one method/approach).
cards.spanishPhrase: Hay un método más fácil para esto.
cards.chinesePhrase: 这件事有更简单的方法。
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Zhè jiàn shì yǒu gèng jiǎndān de fāngfǎ.

cards.grammarNote: Can function as a verb: 生活在北京 = 'to live in Beijing'. As a noun it is uncountable, so no measure word is needed.
cards.spanishPhrase: La vida en esta ciudad es cara.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Zhège chéngshì de shēnghuó hěn guì.

cards.grammarNote: 个 is the measure word: 一个名字. 你叫什么名字？= 'What is your name?'
cards.spanishPhrase: ¿Cuál es tu nombre?
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Nǐ jiào shénme míngzi?

cards.grammarNote: 种 (zhǒng) is the measure word: 一种语言 (one language). Specific languages use the suffix 语 or 文: 中文, 西班牙语.
cards.spanishPhrase: Quiero aprender otro idioma.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ xiǎng zài xué yī mén yǔyán.

cards.grammarNote: 种 (zhǒng) as measure word: 一种文化. Often used in compounds: 文化交流 (cultural exchange).
cards.spanishPhrase: Me interesa mucho la cultura china.
cards.chinesePhrase: 我对中国文化很感兴趣。
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ duì Zhōngguó wénhuà hěn gǎn xìngqù.

cards.grammarNote: 段 (duàn) for a period of history: 一段历史. As an academic subject or general concept, no measure word is needed.
cards.spanishPhrase: La historia de ese país es fascinante.
cards.chinesePhrase: 那个国家的历史很有意思。
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Nàge guójiā de lìshǐ hěn yǒu yìsi.

cards.grammarNote: 个 is the measure word: 一个国家. 国 alone means 'country' in compounds, e.g. 中国, 美国.
cards.spanishPhrase: Ese país tiene una gran economía.
cards.chinesePhrase: 那个国家的经济很发达。
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Nàge guójiā de jīngjì hěn fādá.

cards.grammarNote: 个 is the measure word: 一个社会. Used in compounds: 社会主义 (socialism), 社会问题 (social issue).
cards.spanishPhrase: La sociedad cambia muy rápido.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Shèhuì biànhuà de hěn kuài.

cards.grammarNote: 个 is the measure word: 一个政府. 政府 specifically means the administrative government body.
cards.spanishPhrase: El gobierno anunció nuevas medidas.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Zhèngfǔ xuānbù le xīn cuòshī.

cards.grammarNote: Can function as a verb: 教育孩子 = 'to educate children'. As a noun concept, no measure word is needed.
cards.spanishPhrase: La educación es muy importante.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Jiàoyù fēicháng zhòngyào.

cards.grammarNote: 门 (mén) is used for academic disciplines: 一门科学 (one branch of science). 科学 can also function as an adjective meaning 'scientific'.
cards.spanishPhrase: La ciencia avanza muy rápido.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Kēxué fāzhǎn de hěn kuài.

cards.grammarNote: 项 (xiàng) is a common measure word: 一项技术 (one technology/technique). 技术 emphasizes applied skill; 科技 (kējì) is shorthand for science and technology.
cards.spanishPhrase: La tecnología cambia nuestra vida.
cards.chinesePhrase: 技术改变了我们的生活。
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Jìshù gǎibiàn le wǒmen de shēnghuó.

cards.grammarNote: 个 is the measure word: 一个经济体 (one economy). 经济 can also be an adjective meaning 'economical / affordable'.
cards.spanishPhrase: La economía global está creciendo.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Quánqiú jīngjì zài zēngzhǎng.

cards.grammarNote: 个 is the measure word: 一个市场. Can refer to a physical marketplace or an economic market.
cards.spanishPhrase: Vamos al mercado esta mañana.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒmen jīntiān zǎoshang qù shìchǎng.

cards.grammarNote: 家 (jiā) is the measure word for companies and businesses: 一家公司 (one company).
cards.spanishPhrase: Trabajo en una empresa grande.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ zài yī jiā dà gōngsī gōngzuò.

cards.grammarNote: 条 (tiáo) for individual pieces of information: 一条信息. Also used to mean 'message' in digital contexts (e.g. text message).
cards.spanishPhrase: Necesito más información sobre eso.
cards.chinesePhrase: 我需要更多关于这件事的信息。
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ xūyào gèng duō guānyú zhè jiàn shì de xìnxī.

cards.grammarNote: 条 (tiáo) is the measure word: 一条消息 (one piece of news/message). 新闻 (xīnwén) is more formal 'news'; 消息 is often personal or informal.
cards.spanishPhrase: ¿Escuchaste las noticias de hoy?
cards.chinesePhrase: 你听到今天的消息了吗？
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Nǐ tīng dào jīntiān de xiāoxi le ma?

cards.grammarNote: 种 (zhǒng) is the measure word: 一种能力 (one kind of ability). 有能力 = 'capable / able'.
cards.spanishPhrase: Ella tiene mucha habilidad para los idiomas.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Tā xué yǔyán de nénglì hěn qiáng.

cards.grammarNote: 段 (duàn) or 次 (cì) for a specific experience: 一次经验. As general knowledge from experience, no measure word is needed: 有经验 = 'experienced'.
cards.spanishPhrase: Tiene mucha experiencia en este campo.
cards.chinesePhrase: 他在这个领域有很多经验。
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Tā zài zhège lǐngyù yǒu hěn duō jīngyàn.

cards.grammarNote: 个 is the measure word: 一个机会 (one opportunity). 有机会 = 'to have a chance'. Often paired with 抓住 (zhuāzhù) = 'to seize'.
cards.spanishPhrase: Es una gran oportunidad de aprender.
cards.chinesePhrase: 这是一个很好的学习机会。
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Zhè shì yī gè hěn hǎo de xuéxí jīhuì.

cards.grammarNote: 种 (zhǒng) or 个 as measure word: 一种关系. 没关系 (méi guānxi) = 'it doesn't matter / no problem'.
cards.spanishPhrase: Tenemos una buena relación de trabajo.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒmen de gōngzuò guānxi hěn hǎo.

cards.grammarNote: Can function as both noun and verb: 产生影响 = 'to produce an influence'; 影响我 = 'to influence me'. 种 (zhǒng) as measure word.
cards.spanishPhrase: La música tiene mucha influencia en él.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Yīnyuè duì tā yǐngxiǎng hěn dà.

cards.grammarNote: 种 (zhǒng) or 个 as measure word: 一个变化 (one change). 发生变化 = 'to undergo a change'. Verb form: 变 (biàn) or 改变 (gǎibiàn).
cards.spanishPhrase: Este cambio es muy positivo.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Zhège biànhuà hěn hǎo.

cards.grammarNote: Can be both noun and verb: 促进发展 = 'to promote development'; 发展经济 = 'to develop the economy'. No measure word needed as a general concept.
cards.spanishPhrase: El desarrollo económico fue muy rápido.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Jīngjì fāzhǎn fēicháng kuài.

cards.grammarNote: 个 is the measure word: 一个意思 (one meaning). 什么意思？= 'What does it mean?' 有意思 = 'interesting'.
cards.spanishPhrase: ¿Cuál es el significado de esa palabra?
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Nàge cí shì shénme yìsi?

cards.grammarNote: 个 is the measure word: 一个想法. 有什么想法？= 'Do you have any ideas?' Related: 主意 (zhǔyi) = 'plan/idea (to act on)'.
cards.spanishPhrase: Tengo una idea para el proyecto.
cards.chinesePhrase: 我对这个项目有一个想法。
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ duì zhège xiàngmù yǒu yī gè xiǎngfǎ.

cards.grammarNote: 个 is the measure word: 一个原因 (one reason). 因为...所以... (yīnwèi...suǒyǐ...) = 'because...therefore...' is the standard reason-result pattern.
cards.spanishPhrase: ¿Cuál es la razón de tu decisión?
cards.chinesePhrase: 你做这个决定的原因是什么？
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Nǐ zuò zhège juédìng de yuányīn shì shénme?

cards.grammarNote: 个 is the measure word: 一个结果. Can also function as a conjunction meaning 'as a result / consequently'. 导致结果 = 'to lead to a result'.
cards.spanishPhrase: El resultado fue mejor de lo esperado.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Jiéguǒ bǐ yùqī de hǎo.

cards.grammarNote: 间 (jiān) is the measure word: 一间房间. Note that 间 (jiān) is actually the same character as the second character of 房间.
cards.spanishPhrase: Hay una mesa en mi habitación.
cards.chinesePhrase: 我的房间里有一张桌子。
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ de fángjiān lǐ yǒu yī zhāng zhuōzi.

cards.grammarNote: 间 (jiān) is the measure word: 一间卧室. 卧 means 'to lie down'; 室 means 'room/chamber'.
cards.spanishPhrase: El dormitorio es tranquilo y cómodo.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wòshì ānjìng yòu shūshì.

cards.grammarNote: 间 (jiān) is the measure word: 一间浴室. 浴 means 'to bathe'. 洗手间 (xǐshǒujiān) specifically means restroom/toilet room.
cards.spanishPhrase: El baño está al final del pasillo.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Yùshì zài zǒuláng de jìntóu.

cards.grammarNote: 间 (jiān) is the measure word: 一间客厅. 客 means 'guest'; 厅 means 'hall'. Literally 'guest hall'.
cards.spanishPhrase: Vemos la tele en la sala de estar.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒmen zài kètīng kàn diànshì.

cards.grammarNote: 扇 (shàn) is the measure word for doors and windows: 一扇门. 开门 = 'to open the door'; 关门 = 'to close the door'.
cards.spanishPhrase: Por favor cierra la puerta al salir.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Chūmén de shíhòu qǐng guān mén.

cards.grammarNote: 扇 (shàn) is the measure word: 一扇窗户. 开窗户 = 'to open the window'.
cards.spanishPhrase: Abre la ventana, hace calor.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Tiānqì rè, bǎ chuānghu dǎkāi ba.

cards.grammarNote: 把 (bǎ) is the measure word for chairs: 一把椅子. 坐椅子 = 'to sit in a chair'.
cards.spanishPhrase: Siéntate en esa silla, por favor.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Qǐng zuò zài nà bǎ yǐzi shàng.

cards.grammarNote: 张 (zhāng) is the measure word for beds: 一张床. 上床 = 'to go to bed'; 起床 = 'to get out of bed'.
cards.spanishPhrase: Me gusta leer en la cama.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ xǐhuān zài chuáng shàng kàn shū.

cards.grammarNote: 张 (zhāng) is the measure word: 一张沙发. 沙发 is a phonetic transliteration of the English word 'sofa'.
cards.spanishPhrase: El gato duerme en el sofá.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Māo shuì zài shāfā shàng.

cards.grammarNote: 台 (tái) is the measure word for appliances: 一台冰箱. 冰 = 'ice'; 箱 = 'box/chest'. Literally 'ice box'.
cards.spanishPhrase: Hay fruta fresca en el refrigerador.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Bīngxiāng lǐ yǒu xīnxiān shuǐguǒ.

cards.grammarNote: 台 (tái) is the measure word: 一台空调. Short for 空气调节 (kōngqì tiáojié). 开空调 = 'to turn on the AC'.
cards.spanishPhrase: ¿Puedes encender el aire acondicionado?
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Nǐ néng kāi yīxià kōngtiáo ma?

cards.grammarNote: 部 (bù) or 个 as measure word: 一部电话. 打电话 = 'to make a phone call'. 接电话 = 'to answer the phone'.
cards.spanishPhrase: El teléfono sonó dos veces.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Diànhuà xiǎng le liǎng cì.

cards.grammarNote: 部 (bù) is the measure word: 一部手机. 手 = 'hand'; 机 = 'machine'. Literally 'hand machine'.
cards.spanishPhrase: Olvidé mi celular en casa.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ bǎ shǒujī wàng zài jiā lǐ le.

cards.grammarNote: 台 (tái) is the measure word: 一台电脑. 电 = 'electric'; 脑 = 'brain'. Literally 'electric brain'. Also 计算机 (jìsuànjī) in formal/technical contexts.
cards.spanishPhrase: Trabajo en la computadora todo el día.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ zhěng tiān yòng diànnǎo gōngzuò.

cards.grammarNote: 条 (tiáo) or 个 as measure word: 一条网络. 上网络 = 'to go online on a network'. Note: 互联网 specifically means 'the Internet (WWW)'.
cards.spanishPhrase: La red es muy lenta hoy.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Jīntiān wǎngluò hěn màn.

cards.grammarNote: 台 (tái) is the measure word: 一台电视. 看电视 = 'to watch TV'. 电视节目 = 'TV program'.
cards.spanishPhrase: Apaga la televisión y duerme.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Guān diào diànshì qù shuìjiào ba.

cards.grammarNote: 张 (zhāng) is the measure word: 一张照片. 拍照片 = 'to take a photo'. Also shortened to just 照 in casual speech.
cards.spanishPhrase: Saqué muchas fotos en el viaje.
cards.chinesePhrase: 我在旅行中拍了很多照片。
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ zài lǚxíng zhōng pāi le hěn duō zhàopiàn.

cards.grammarNote: 个 or 段 (duàn) as measure word: 一段视频 (one video clip). 看视频 = 'to watch a video'. 直播视频 = 'live video'.
cards.spanishPhrase: Mira este video, es muy divertido.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Kàn zhège shìpín, hěn yǒu yìsi.

cards.grammarNote: No measure word needed when referring to the Internet as a whole. 上互联网 = 'to go on the Internet'. Often shortened to 网 in everyday speech.
cards.spanishPhrase: Busco información en internet.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ zài hùliánwǎng shàng chá xìnxī.

cards.grammarNote: 个 is the measure word: 一个密码. 输入密码 = 'to enter a password'. 忘记密码 = 'to forget a password'.
cards.spanishPhrase: Olvidé mi contraseña otra vez.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ yòu wàngjì mìmǎ le.

cards.grammarNote: 个 or 款 (kuǎn) as measure word: 一款应用程序. Commonly shortened to 应用 (yìngyòng) or 程序 (chéngxù). The informal term is APP (read as āi pī pī).
cards.spanishPhrase: Descargué una nueva aplicación ayer.
cards.chinesePhrase: 我昨天下载了一个新应用程序。
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ zuótiān xiàzài le yī gè xīn yìngyòng chéngxù.

cards.grammarNote: 辆 (liàng) is the measure word for wheeled vehicles: 一辆车. 坐车 = 'to ride/take a vehicle'. 车 alone often implies a car in modern usage.
cards.spanishPhrase: Ese vehículo es muy grande.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Nà liàng chē hěn dà.

cards.grammarNote: 辆 (liàng) is the measure word: 一辆自行车. 骑自行车 = 'to ride a bicycle'. 自行 means 'self-propelled'.
cards.spanishPhrase: Voy al trabajo en bicicleta.
cards.chinesePhrasePin: Wǒ qí zìxíngchē qù shàngbān.

cards.grammarNote: 辆 (liàng) is the measure word: 一辆摩托车. 摩托 is a phonetic transliteration of 'motor'. 骑摩托车 = 'to ride a motorcycle'.
cards.spanishPhrase: Su moto es roja y rápida.
cards.chinesePhrase: 他的摩托车是红色的，跑得很快。
